title: Moonweave generated canonical Concept payload schemas

CommandOutputObservation.title: command output observation payload
CommandOutputObservation.description: As an execution observation, Command output observation is distinguished by the fact that it records observable command output as context-ingress evidence without owning the command execution or container that produced it.

observation_id.title: observation ID
observation_id.description: Stable identifier for one observation of an execution result.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the observation record was formed.

ContextIngressEvent.title: context ingress event payload
ContextIngressEvent.description: Context ingress event is an event whose defining condition is that it is an observable event in which a message, instruction, source span, retrieved candidate, tool observation, execution output, or summarized artifact becomes available to a model call or agent step.

EnvironmentBindingReference.title: environment binding reference payload
EnvironmentBindingReference.description: Environment binding reference is an execution reference; its differentiating condition is that it references a runtime environment binding relevant to observed execution output without exposing hidden or secret values by default.

ExecutionObservation.title: execution observation payload
ExecutionObservation.description: As an information record, Execution observation is distinguished by the fact that it is identity-bearing information with observation time, a reference to the observed execution, visible payload, and source evidence. It records permitted observable execution phenomena without owning the command or execution activity.

observation_id.title: observation ID
observation_id.description: Stable identifier for one observation of an execution result.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the observation record was formed.

ExecutionReference.title: execution reference payload
ExecutionReference.description: Execution reference is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is information with a reference identifier, reference kind, target system, and visibility constraints. It points to execution-related objects without copying them or exposing hidden values.

ExecutionResultReference.title: execution result reference payload
ExecutionResultReference.description: Execution result reference is an execution reference whose defining condition is that it references a runtime or tool execution result whose observable output or status is being staged into context.

ExitStatusObservation.title: exit status observation payload
ExitStatusObservation.description: Exit status observation is an execution observation whose defining condition is that it represents an exit status after it has been observed and staged as context evidence for an agent step.

observation_id.title: observation ID
observation_id.description: Stable identifier for one observation of an execution result.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the observation record was formed.

exit_status.title: command exit status
exit_status.description: An exit code or termination status is a scalar observed value of ExitStatusObservation without independent business identity; a field can still record code, signal, and reason.

OutputChunk.title: output chunk payload
OutputChunk.description: Output chunk is an output segment whose defining condition is that it represents a bounded segment of command, tool, model, process, or artifact output prepared for citation, truncation, disclosure, or later context staging.

OutputSegment.title: output segment payload
OutputSegment.description: As an output information, Output segment is distinguished by the fact that it records an addressable segment within an output stream with ordering, source, visibility, and optional citation metadata.

OutputStream.title: output stream payload
OutputStream.description: Output stream belongs to the collection category and differs from broader members in that it records an ordered stream of output segments produced by a model, command, process, or tool before windowing or disclosure.

StandardError.title: standard error stream payload
StandardError.description: Standard error stream is an output stream; its differentiating condition is that it captures the stderr stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable diagnostic output.

StandardErrorChunk.title: standard error chunk payload
StandardErrorChunk.description: As an output chunk, Standard error chunk is distinguished by the fact that it represents a bounded stderr segment with ordering, diagnostic meaning, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.

StandardOutput.title: standard output stream payload
StandardOutput.description: Standard output stream is an output stream whose defining condition is that it captures the stdout stream emitted by a command, process, sandbox, or tool execution as observable output available for staging into context.

StandardOutputChunk.title: standard output chunk payload
StandardOutputChunk.description: Standard output chunk is an output chunk; its differentiating condition is that it represents a bounded stdout segment with ordering, source span, truncation, and context-selection metadata.

WorkingDirectoryReference.title: working directory reference payload
WorkingDirectoryReference.description: Working directory reference belongs to the execution reference category and differs from broader members in that it references the working directory relevant to observed execution output without making the directory itself a context-ingress artifact.

AudioBlock.title: audio block payload
AudioBlock.description: Audio block belongs to the content block category and differs from broader members in that it represents audio content or transcribed audio observations staged into context with modality and source metadata.

encoding.title: encoding
encoding.description: Character or binary encoding used to interpret the content bytes.

modality.title: message modality
modality.description: Modality is a controlled classification dimension of ContentBlock; concrete text, image, and audio values are expressed by the field together with strict block subtypes, not a shadow node.

mime_type.title: media type
mime_type.description: Internet media type of the content; it is a field rather than a graph node.

token_count.title: token count
token_count.description: Token count is a numeric measurement relative to a tokenizer; as a ContentBlock field it can carry count and tokenizer version without becoming a graph node.

block_id.title: content-block ID
block_id.description: Stable identifier for the block within its containing message or content package.

CodeBlock.title: code block payload
CodeBlock.description: Code block is a content block whose defining condition is that it represents source code, patches, stack traces, shell snippets, or executable text staged into context.

FileAttachmentBlock.title: file attachment block payload
FileAttachmentBlock.description: As a content block, File attachment block is distinguished by the fact that it represents an attached file or file excerpt made visible to a model call, including digest, mime type, and access reference.

MessageContentBlock.title: message content association payload
MessageContentBlock.description: Message content association is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it links a message envelope to one or more typed content blocks so multimodal content can be cited, filtered, or budgeted.

StructuredDataBlock.title: structured data block payload
StructuredDataBlock.description: Structured data block is a content block whose defining condition is that it represents JSON, records, schemas, tables, graph fragments, or other structured payloads staged into context.

CandidateRank.title: candidate rank observation payload
CandidateRank.description: Candidate rank observation belongs to the quality characteristic category and differs from broader members in that it is an observed quality of ordinal position in a particular discovery run, not a subtype of index, result, or candidate; it remains a concept to carry method and evidence.

DiscoveryActivity.title: Discovery activity payload
DiscoveryActivity.description: Discovery activity belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it examines discoverable surfaces for a query or requirement and produces candidates that still require selection; it neither makes the final selection nor owns a persistent index.

DiscoveryIndex.title: discovery index payload
DiscoveryIndex.description: As an information record, Discovery index is distinguished by the fact that it is an identity-bearing, queryable information artifact with build or refresh time, coverage, and version reference for finding context candidates. It does not own Memory's persistent-index storage, shards, or lifecycle.

DiscoveryResult.title: discovery result payload
DiscoveryResult.description: Discovery result is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is information with result identity, a reference to the producing query or surface, source reference, optional score, and selection status. Being a result does not imply inclusion in a ContextPackage.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

DiscoveryScore.title: discovery score payload
DiscoveryScore.description: As a quality characteristic, Discovery score is distinguished by the fact that it is an observed quality of a discovery result on relevance, similarity, lexical-match, or reranking scales. It records method, scale, direction, time, and scored subject; it is neither a result nor an index.

DiscoverySurface.title: discovery surface payload
DiscoverySurface.description: Discovery surface belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it identifies a lightweight surface such as search, file listing, memory namespace, tool descriptor list, or protocol resource list used to discover context candidates.

IndexPointer.title: index pointer payload
IndexPointer.description: Index pointer belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it points from a context selection process to an index entry, search surface, or retrieval handle that may produce candidate content.

IndexVersionReference.title: index version reference payload
IndexVersionReference.description: Index version reference belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it references the index version, snapshot, build, or configuration that produced a discovery candidate.

LightIndex.title: lightweight discovery index payload
LightIndex.description: As a discovery index, Lightweight discovery index is distinguished by the fact that it represents a lightweight discovery surface or pointer set used to find candidate context without owning the full indexing system.

LightweightRetrieval.title: Lightweight retrieval payload
LightweightRetrieval.description: Lightweight retrieval is a discovery activity that reads an index view or pointer in the current context session and returns candidates without rebuilding a persistent index.

LightweightRetrievalTrace.title: lightweight retrieval trace payload
LightweightRetrievalTrace.description: Lightweight retrieval trace belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records observable query, candidate, score, rank, and source-reference evidence for a lightweight context discovery pass.

SearchResult.title: search result payload
SearchResult.description: Search result belongs to the discovery result category and differs from broader members in that it records a candidate returned by a search or discovery surface, including source reference, score, rank, and selection status.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

SearchScore.title: search score payload
SearchScore.description: Search score is a discovery score whose defining condition is that it records the score attached to a retrieved candidate, search result, index hit, or source pointer during context discovery.

AssistantMessage.title: assistant message payload
AssistantMessage.description: Assistant message is a message; its differentiating condition is that it records an assistant-authored message envelope that may contain model output, tool-call intent, citations, or disclosed reasoning summaries.

receiver_id.title: message receiver
receiver_id.description: The receiver is a Message reference field to an Actor, model call, or tool; a role occurrence should not be an independent type node.

message_role.title: message role
message_role.description: System, developer, user, assistant, and tool are controlled Message classification values; stable genera are already expressed by Message subtypes, so no shadow role node is retained.

sender_id.title: message sender
sender_id.description: The sender is a Message reference field to an Actor, service, tool, or user, not a reusable subject type.

reply_to_id.title: reply-to message identifier
reply_to_id.description: A message may reference the stable identifier of the message it replies to. This relational scalar has no independent ontological identity, so it becomes Message.reply_to_id while the Message --replies_to→ Message type relation declares its endpoint semantics.

message_id.title: message ID
message_id.description: Stable identifier for the message within its protocol or conversation scope.

conversation_id.title: conversation ID
conversation_id.description: Identifier value correlating the containing conversation, not a separate Concept node.

sequence_index.title: sequence index
sequence_index.description: Protocol-defined or zero-based position of the message in its conversation order.

correlation_id.title: correlation ID
correlation_id.description: Optional identifier correlating requests, responses, and related events.

Conversation.title: conversation payload
Conversation.description: A Conversation is a collection that groups ordered message turns, instructions, tool observations, and context packages under one dialogue or thread identity.

conversation_id.title: conversation identifier
conversation_id.description: A conversation identifier is an identity key of Conversation with no independent ontological lifecycle, so it becomes a required structural field.

thread_id.title: thread identifier
thread_id.description: A thread identifier is a Conversation correlation key for resumption or task grouping and has no independent ontological identity, so it becomes a field.

ConversationElement.title: conversation structure element payload
ConversationElement.description: Conversation structure element belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that conversationElement is identity-bearing information participating in conversation structure. Its subtypes cover the full conversation, an ordered turn, and retained history; it does not prescribe sender role or message payload.

ConversationTurn.title: conversation turn payload
ConversationTurn.description: As a conversation structure element, Conversation turn is distinguished by the fact that it identifies one ordered exchange position in a conversation and binds message content to turn index, role, sender, and reply context.

turn_index.title: turn index
turn_index.description: Turn index is an ordinal value on ConversationTurn, not an index-type concept; ordering is additionally validated by precedes.

ProtocolEnvelope.title: protocol envelope payload
ProtocolEnvelope.description: As an information record, Protocol envelope is distinguished by the fact that it captures protocol-level wrapping around a message, including task identity, sender, receiver, artifact references, and capability metadata without promoting adapter-specific fields into core.
