ContextSelectionDecision.title: context selection decision payload
ContextSelectionDecision.description: Context selection decision belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records candidate output, visible window, policy basis, and intended disclosure outcome; it governs disclosure activity but does not itself select, truncate, or suppress content.

DisclosedOutputSegment.title: disclosed output segment payload
DisclosedOutputSegment.description: Disclosed output segment is a disclosure audit artifact whose defining condition is that it represents an output segment that has been made visible to a recipient or downstream context together with citation and boundary metadata.

DisclosureActivity.title: Output disclosure activity payload
DisclosureActivity.description: Output disclosure activity is an activity whose defining condition is that it applies selection, truncation, suppression, or publication to runtime output under audience, visible-window, and safety decisions and produces a disclosure record.

DisclosureArtifact.title: disclosure audit artifact payload
DisclosureArtifact.description: Disclosure audit artifact is an information record; its differentiating condition is that disclosureArtifact is identity-bearing information with the producing selection or disclosure activity, recipient, boundary, policy basis, and handled-content reference. It audits disclosure outcomes and is not the disclosure policy or activity.

DisclosurePublicationActivity.title: Disclosure publication activity payload
DisclosurePublicationActivity.description: As an Output disclosure activity, Disclosure publication activity is distinguished by the fact that it is a Disclosure publication releases the allowed final output segments to the target audience and trust boundary.

DisclosureRecord.title: Output disclosure record payload
DisclosureRecord.description: As an information record, Output disclosure record is distinguished by the fact that it is auditable information for one disclosure activity, recording target audience, content references, applicable decisions, and a shown, truncated, or suppressed outcome.

outcome_code.title: disclosure outcome
outcome_code.description: Records one controlled disclosure outcome: shown, truncated, or suppressed.

DisclosureSelectionActivity.title: Disclosure selection activity payload
DisclosureSelectionActivity.description: As an Output disclosure activity, Disclosure selection activity is distinguished by the fact that it determines which output segments are eligible for a target audience.

DisclosureStage.title: disclosure stage activity payload
DisclosureStage.description: records a step in which output or context content is revealed, withheld, escalated, redacted, or released across a boundary.

DisclosureSuppressionActivity.title: Disclosure suppression activity payload
DisclosureSuppressionActivity.description: Disclosure suppression activity belongs to the Output disclosure activity category and differs from broader members in that it is a Disclosure suppression prevents content that fails authorization or safety conditions from reaching the target audience.

DisclosureTruncationActivity.title: Disclosure truncation activity payload
DisclosureTruncationActivity.description: Disclosure truncation activity belongs to the Output disclosure activity category and differs from broader members in that Disclosure truncation shortens selected content to window or policy bounds while retaining omitted ranges.

HeadTailWindow.title: head-tail output window payload
HeadTailWindow.description: As an output window, Head-tail output window is distinguished by the fact that it retains the beginning and ending spans of long output so important setup and terminal evidence can remain visible under length limits.

length_limit.title: length limit
length_limit.description: Maximum characters, bytes, or tokens admitted by the window, interpreted by unit.

unit.title: unit
unit.description: Controlled measurement unit used by length_limit.

OutputCitation.title: output citation payload
OutputCitation.description: As an output information, Output citation is distinguished by the fact that it links visible output or a disclosed segment to the source span, retrieval candidate, trace event, or artifact that supports it.

OutputInformation.title: output information payload
OutputInformation.description: Output information is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is the root for output-related information with source, producer, order or extent, and visibility. Subtypes may be stream collections, addressable segments, visible-range specifications, or evidence citations, but not the activity producing output.

OutputWindow.title: output window payload
OutputWindow.description: Output window belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it identifies the visible or retained range of output that may be shown, cited, summarized, or passed into later context.

length_limit.title: length limit
length_limit.description: Maximum characters, bytes, or tokens admitted by the window, interpreted by unit.

unit.title: unit
unit.description: Controlled measurement unit used by length_limit.

SuppressedContext.title: suppressed context payload
SuppressedContext.description: Suppressed context is a disclosure audit artifact; its differentiating condition is that it records content intentionally kept out of visible context because of policy, trust boundary, relevance, privacy, or budget constraints.

TruncatedContextSpan.title: truncated context span payload
TruncatedContextSpan.description: Truncated context span belongs to the disclosure audit artifact category and differs from broader members in that it records the portion of content removed or shortened by a context window, length limit, summarization, or output clipping decision.

FewShotExample.title: few-shot example payload
FewShotExample.description: As an information record, Few-shot example is distinguished by the fact that it stores an example input-output or demonstration pattern used to condition behavior while remaining traceable as context content.

Instruction.title: instruction payload
Instruction.description: Instruction is a specification whose defining condition is that it records a directive that constrains agent behavior and carries authority, scope, priority, provenance, and conflict-resolution metadata.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: captures priority as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

InstructionApplicability.title: instruction applicability payload
InstructionApplicability.description: Instruction applicability belongs to the instruction metadata category and differs from broader members in that it states the conditions under which an instruction is active, inactive, inherited, or limited for a context package or agent step.

InstructionAuthority.title: instruction authority payload
InstructionAuthority.description: Instruction authority is an instruction metadata; its differentiating condition is that it states the authority level and source of an instruction so conflicts among system, developer, user, policy, retrieved, or tool-originated directives can be resolved.

InstructionConflictDetectionActivity.title: Instruction conflict-detection activity payload
InstructionConflictDetectionActivity.description: As an Instruction processing, Instruction conflict-detection activity is distinguished by the fact that it compares authority, priority, scope, and content of parsed instructions and references safety-owned conflict records.

InstructionMetadata.title: instruction metadata payload
InstructionMetadata.description: Instruction metadata is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is information associated with one or more Instructions and carrying its own provenance and validity time. It explains when, why, for whom, and at what precedence an instruction applies; it is not the directive content itself.

InstructionOverride.title: instruction override payload
InstructionOverride.description: Instruction override is an instruction whose defining condition is that it records an authorized replacement or narrowing of one instruction by another with higher authority or more specific scope.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: captures priority as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

InstructionParsingActivity.title: Instruction parsing activity payload
InstructionParsingActivity.description: Instruction parsing activity is an Instruction processing; its differentiating condition is that it identifies instruction boundaries, role provenance, and metadata in a prompt instance without resolving conflicts.

InstructionPriority.title: instruction priority payload
InstructionPriority.description: As an instruction metadata, Instruction priority is distinguished by the fact that it records the precedence ordering used when multiple applicable instructions compete for control of a model call or agent step.

InstructionProcessing.title: Instruction processing payload
InstructionProcessing.description: Instruction processing is the activity that parses instructions from a prompt instance, detects conflicts, and produces a resolution using authority, priority, and scope.

InstructionProvenance.title: instruction provenance payload
InstructionProvenance.description: Instruction provenance is an instruction metadata; its differentiating condition is that it links an instruction to its source message, policy, prompt template, retrieved artifact, protocol envelope, or human approval record.

InstructionResolution.title: instruction resolution payload
InstructionResolution.description: Instruction resolution is an information record whose defining condition is that it records the selected outcome for an instruction conflict, including which directive was followed, overridden, ignored, narrowed, or escalated.

InstructionResolutionActivity.title: Instruction resolution activity payload
InstructionResolutionActivity.description: Instruction resolution activity is an Instruction processing; its differentiating condition is that it applies explicit rules to conflicts and overrides and produces a result stating effective instructions and rationale.

InstructionScope.title: instruction scope payload
InstructionScope.description: As an instruction metadata, Instruction scope is distinguished by the fact that it bounds where an instruction applies, such as session, task, tool call, retrieved document, output format, or trust boundary.

PromptTemplate.title: prompt template payload
PromptTemplate.description: Prompt template is a prompt definition whose defining condition is that it is a templated message or workflow pattern whose variables can be bound during prompt instantiation before being staged into a model call or agent step.

PromptTemplateInstance.title: prompt template instance payload
PromptTemplateInstance.description: A PromptTemplateInstance is an information record that stores one filled prompt template with bindings, source references, and authority metadata used in a context package.

variable_bindings.title: prompt variable binding
variable_bindings.description: A prompt variable is a repeatable name-value-source binding within a template instance; a field expresses it fully when it has no independent business identity.

SystemPrompt.title: system prompt instruction payload
SystemPrompt.description: System prompt instruction is an instruction; its differentiating condition is that it captures high-authority system-level instruction content that frames model behavior for a session, task, or model call.

priority.title: Priority
priority.description: captures priority as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

AccessPath.title: access path payload
AccessPath.description: Access path is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it describes the path or handle through which a source reference can be retrieved, subject to permission, policy, and trust boundary controls.

CitationAnchor.title: citation anchor payload
CitationAnchor.description: Citation anchor is a source anchor whose defining condition is that it binds a visible citation to the exact source reference and span that support a message, output, summary, or artifact claim.

ContentDigest.title: content digest payload
ContentDigest.description: Content digest is a source identity information; its differentiating condition is that it records a compact fingerprint of content used for deduplication, integrity checks, cache validation, or citation stability.

DatabaseRowReference.title: database row reference payload
DatabaseRowReference.description: As a source reference, Database row reference is distinguished by the fact that it references a specific database row, query result, or table slice that can support context content without owning the database.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

DataZoneReference.title: data zone reference payload
DataZoneReference.description: Data zone reference belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it points from a context artifact to the data zone whose visibility, retention, and handling policy governs that artifact.

FileResourceReference.title: file resource reference payload
FileResourceReference.description: File resource reference is a source reference; its differentiating condition is that it references a file-like resource by path, URI, version, digest, or span without modeling the full file system as context input.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

GraphNodeReference.title: graph node reference payload
GraphNodeReference.description: Graph node reference is a source reference; its differentiating condition is that it references a graph node or graph-local identifier that can support context content without owning the graph store.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

NetworkSourceReference.title: network source reference payload
NetworkSourceReference.description: Network source reference is a source reference whose defining condition is that it references network-originated content or an endpoint-derived source while preserving trust-boundary and access-path metadata.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

ResourceDescriptor.title: resource descriptor payload
ResourceDescriptor.description: Resource descriptor is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it summarizes identity, type, provenance, location, access hints, and trust metadata for a source or resource reference staged into context.

SourceAnchor.title: source anchor payload
SourceAnchor.description: As an information record, Source anchor is distinguished by the fact that it provides a stable anchor that can re-identify a source span after chunking, rendering, indexing, or document revision.

SourceCellRange.title: source cell range payload
SourceCellRange.description: Source cell range is a source location whose defining condition is that it records table, sheet, notebook, or matrix cell ranges that support context content or citations.

SourceChecksum.title: source checksum payload
SourceChecksum.description: As a content digest, Source checksum is distinguished by the fact that it records a digest or checksum used to detect whether cited source material has changed since ingestion or context staging.

SourceIdentity.title: source identity information payload
SourceIdentity.description: Source identity information is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is information associated with a SourceReference to identify a concrete source state when location remains the same but content changes. It may use version identifiers, content digests, or checksums and must state the algorithm or identifier scheme.

SourceLineRange.title: source line range payload
SourceLineRange.description: Source line range is a source location whose defining condition is that it records line-based source span boundaries used for code, logs, terminal output, documents, or text files.

SourceLocation.title: source location payload
SourceLocation.description: Source location is an information record whose defining condition is that it records a source position such as URI, file path, database locator, graph identifier, page, line, offset, or cell reference.

SourceOffset.title: source offset payload
SourceOffset.description: As a source location, Source offset is distinguished by the fact that it records byte, character, token, or structural offsets used to locate a source span precisely.

SourcePageRange.title: source page range payload
SourcePageRange.description: Source page range is a source location; its differentiating condition is that it records page-based source span boundaries used for paginated documents, PDFs, or rendered reports.

SourceProvenance.title: source provenance payload
SourceProvenance.description: Source provenance is an information record whose defining condition is that it links a source reference to acquisition, ingestion, transformation, trust boundary, and evidence metadata.

SourceReference.title: source reference payload
SourceReference.description: As an information record, Source reference is distinguished by the fact that it identifies a cited or retrievable source object by stable reference without claiming ownership of the storage system that hosts it.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

SourceSpan.title: source span payload
SourceSpan.description: Source span is a source location; its differentiating condition is that it identifies the precise portion of a source that supports a context block, retrieval candidate, citation, summary, or disclosed output segment.

SourceVersion.title: source version payload
SourceVersion.description: Source version is a source identity information; its differentiating condition is that it records the version, revision, timestamp, commit, snapshot, or release identifier associated with a source reference.

TextDocumentReference.title: text document reference payload
TextDocumentReference.description: Text document reference is a source reference; its differentiating condition is that it references a text document as source evidence while keeping the document store and ingestion system outside the context-ingress owner domain.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

TrustBoundaryReference.title: trust boundary reference payload
TrustBoundaryReference.description: Trust boundary reference belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it points from a context artifact to the trust boundary crossed or relied on by its source, transport, retrieval, or disclosure path.

AnswerOwnership.title: answer ownership payload
AnswerOwnership.description: Answer ownership is a coordination ownership record; its differentiating condition is that it records which coordinating actor is accountable for the final response or artifact after delegation, handoff, review, or synthesis.

ControlOwnership.title: control ownership payload
ControlOwnership.description: As a coordination ownership record, Control ownership is distinguished by the fact that it records which actor currently controls the next workflow step, including whether control is retained by a manager or transferred through handoff.

CoordinationOwnershipRecord.title: coordination ownership record payload
CoordinationOwnershipRecord.description: Coordination ownership record is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it identifies, at a time during delegation, handoff, or synthesis, which actor owns answer accountability, next-step control, or delegation responsibility, with scope, validity, and transfer provenance.

CoordinationRole.title: coordination role payload
CoordinationRole.description: Coordination role is a role; its differentiating condition is that it specifies decomposition, delegation, performance, supervision, synthesis, or reporting responsibilities in orchestration and is borne by an actor through an ActorRoleBinding.

Orchestrator.title: orchestrator role payload
Orchestrator.description: As a coordination role, Orchestrator role is distinguished by the fact that orchestrator denotes decomposition, delegation, routing, and synthesis responsibilities and is therefore a CoordinationRole borne by actors, not a fixed actor subtype.

SubagentRole.title: subagent role payload
SubagentRole.description: Subagent role is a coordination role; its differentiating condition is that it is a CoordinationRole distinguished by bounded work, authority, context, and upward reporting.

WorkerAgent.title: worker role payload
WorkerAgent.description: Worker denotes responsibility for receiving scoped work and returning evidence and can be borne by an agent, model service, or tool service, so it is retyped as a CoordinationRole.

WorkerRole.title: Worker role payload
WorkerRole.description: Worker role is a coordination role that accepts scoped work, performs agreed tasks, and returns evidence; it may be borne by an agent, model service, or tool service.

AggregationActivity.title: Composition aggregation activity payload
AggregationActivity.description: Composition aggregation activity belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it consumes multiple component inputs under a composition pattern and aggregation rule and produces output retaining provenance and decision evidence.

AggregationRule.title: aggregation rule payload
AggregationRule.description: As a specification, Aggregation rule is distinguished by the fact that it is a composition rule that governs how parallel outputs, votes, section results, or evidence fragments are merged into a synthesis.

ChainStage.title: chain stage payload
ChainStage.description: Chain stage is a composition part; its differentiating condition is that it is one ordered stage in a prompt chain with explicit input, output, dependency, guard, and handoff or routing context.

CompositionOutput.title: Composition output payload
CompositionOutput.description: Composition output belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is information produced by an aggregation, voting, or synthesis activity that retains component inputs, pattern, rules, and decision evidence.

CompositionPart.title: composition part payload
CompositionPart.description: Composition part is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a structural specification belonging to an orchestration topology or pattern with local inputs/outputs, sequential or concurrent position, assignment scope, and fan-in responsibility.

CompositionPattern.title: composition pattern payload
CompositionPattern.description: Composition pattern is a specification whose defining condition is that it is a static specification of stage or branch organization, sequential or concurrent constraints, input/output interfaces, fan-in, and failure policy that an orchestration topology can use.

OrchestrationTopology.title: orchestration topology payload
OrchestrationTopology.description: Orchestration topology is a specification whose defining condition is that it describes the coordination shape of a workflow, such as centralized manager-worker, hierarchical crew, graph route, parallel branch, or dynamic swarm.

topology_id.title: topology ID
topology_id.description: Stable identifier for the static orchestration topology across revisions.

version.title: topology version
version.description: Identifies one immutable revision of the topology specification.

failure_policy.title: failure policy
failure_policy.description: States how the topology stops, retries, or degrades when a part fails.

ParallelBranch.title: parallel branch payload
ParallelBranch.description: Parallel branch is a composition part; its differentiating condition is that it is one branch in a parallelized workflow with its own assigned work, context slice, output, and merge expectations.

Parallelization.title: parallelization payload
Parallelization.description: Parallelization is a composition pattern; its differentiating condition is that it is a CompositionPattern distinguished by concurrent branch execution and fan-in.

PromptChain.title: prompt chain payload
PromptChain.description: Prompt chain belongs to the composition pattern category and differs from broader members in that it is a CompositionPattern distinguished by an ordered sequence of prompt or model-call stages.

SectionAssignment.title: section assignment payload
SectionAssignment.description: Section assignment is a composition part whose defining condition is that it is an assignment record connecting a section of work to a worker, context slice, expected output, and synthesis role.

Sectioning.title: sectioning payload
Sectioning.description: Sectioning is a composition pattern; its differentiating condition is that it is a CompositionPattern distinguished by dividing larger work or artifacts into independently handled sections.

Synthesis.title: synthesis payload
Synthesis.description: Synthesis is a Composition aggregation activity (AggregationActivity) that combines multiple inputs, evidence items, or intermediate results into a new conclusion, artifact, or decision while preserving input-to-output traceability.

SynthesisInput.title: synthesis input payload
SynthesisInput.description: Synthesis input is an information record whose defining condition is that it is a worker output, retrieved evidence, tool result, vote, review signal, or artifact fragment consumed by a synthesis step.

SynthesisOutput.title: synthesis output payload
SynthesisOutput.description: Synthesis output is a Composition output; its differentiating condition is that it is a coherent artifact, answer, plan, summary, or decision produced by a synthesis step with provenance back to its inputs.

SynthesisPlan.title: synthesis plan payload
SynthesisPlan.description: Synthesis plan belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is a plan describing which inputs, votes, sections, evidence, and precedence rules a synthesis step must combine.

VoteBallot.title: vote ballot payload
VoteBallot.description: As an information record, Vote ballot is distinguished by the fact that it is a record of a worker, evaluator, model, or rule casting a choice, score, preference, or rationale summary in a voting workflow.

Voting.title: voting payload
Voting.description: Voting is a composition pattern; its differentiating condition is that it is a CompositionPattern distinguished by collecting multiple judgments and selecting or aggregating them under a rule.

VotingActivity.title: Voting aggregation activity payload
VotingActivity.description: As a Composition aggregation activity, Voting aggregation activity is distinguished by the fact that it consumes ballots under a voting composition pattern, applies counting and tie rules, and produces composition output.

AcceptancePhase.title: Acceptance phase payload
AcceptancePhase.description: As a Delegation phase, Acceptance phase is distinguished by the fact that the delegate explicitly accepts the delegation contract and assumes the agreed responsibility.

CollaborationProcess.title: Collaboration process payload
CollaborationProcess.description: A collaboration process is an activity in which two or more participants coordinate responsibility, control, or deliverables around a shared work objective.

CompletionPhase.title: Completion phase payload
CompletionPhase.description: Completion phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegate returns the agreed result and evidence, closing the delegated responsibility under the contract.

ContextIsolation.title: Delegation context-isolation specification payload
ContextIsolation.description: Delegation context-isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a boundary specification that declares the messages, memory, tools, and sources visible to or excluded from one delegation; it is neither sandbox isolation nor the delegated context content itself.

isolation_spec_id.title: isolation-spec ID
isolation_spec_id.description: Records the isolation-spec ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a context isolation instance.

version.title: version
version.description: Records the version needed to identify, validate, or replay a context isolation instance.

allowed_surface_refs.title: allowed-surface references
allowed_surface_refs.description: Records the allowed-surface references needed to identify, validate, or replay a context isolation instance.

denied_surface_refs.title: denied-surface references
denied_surface_refs.description: Records the denied-surface references needed to identify, validate, or replay a context isolation instance.

default_decision.title: default decision
default_decision.description: Records the default decision needed to identify, validate, or replay a context isolation instance.

valid_from.title: valid from
valid_from.description: Records the valid from needed to identify, validate, or replay a context isolation instance.

DelegatedAuthority.title: delegated authority payload
DelegatedAuthority.description: As a specification, Delegated authority is distinguished by the fact that it states the permissions, tools, memory surfaces, data zones, and operations a delegate may use on behalf of the orchestrator.

authority_id.title: authority ID
authority_id.description: Records the authority ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

version.title: version
version.description: Records the version needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

delegatee_id.title: delegatee ID
delegatee_id.description: Records the delegatee ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

permission_refs.title: permission references
permission_refs.description: Records the permission references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

resource_scope_refs.title: resource-scope references
resource_scope_refs.description: Records the resource-scope references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

approved_by.title: approved by
approved_by.description: Records the approved by needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

valid_from.title: valid from
valid_from.description: Records the valid from needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Records the valid until needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegated authority instance.

DelegationBudget.title: delegation budget payload
DelegationBudget.description: Delegation budget is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it sets token, time, cost, retry, tool-call, or context limits that constrain delegated work and routing choices.

budget_id.title: budget ID
budget_id.description: Records the budget ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

version.title: version
version.description: Records the version needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

token_limit.title: token limit
token_limit.description: Records the token limit needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

time_limit_ms.title: time limit in milliseconds
time_limit_ms.description: Records the time limit in milliseconds needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

cost_limit.title: cost limit
cost_limit.description: Records the cost limit needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

retry_limit.title: retry limit
retry_limit.description: Records the retry limit needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

tool_call_limit.title: tool-call limit
tool_call_limit.description: Records the tool-call limit needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

context_token_limit.title: context-token limit
context_token_limit.description: Records the context-token limit needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

approved_by.title: approved by
approved_by.description: Records the approved by needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

valid_from.title: valid from
valid_from.description: Records the valid from needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Records the valid until needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation budget instance.

DelegationContext.title: Delegation context payload
DelegationContext.description: Delegation context belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is bounded context information formed under one delegation contract and context-isolation specification, recording the task state, messages, memory, tools, and sources required and authorized for delegated work.

context_id.title: context ID
context_id.description: Records the context ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

version.title: version
version.description: Records the version needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

delegation_id.title: delegation ID
delegation_id.description: Records the delegation ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

content_digest.title: content digest
content_digest.description: Records the content digest needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

allowed_reference_ids.title: allowed references
allowed_reference_ids.description: Records the allowed references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

excluded_reference_ids.title: excluded references
excluded_reference_ids.description: Records the excluded references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: Records the created at needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

DelegationContract.title: delegation contract payload
DelegationContract.description: Delegation contract is a specification whose defining condition is that it specifies delegated work, expected result, authority scope, visible context, budget, completion criteria, and ownership boundaries between delegator and delegatee.

delegation_id.title: delegation ID
delegation_id.description: Stable identifier for one delegation contract among delegator, delegatee, and work scope.

status.title: delegation status
status.description: State in the proposed, accepted, active, completed, or revoked delegation lifecycle.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: UTC instant at which the delegation contract was formed.

delegator_id.title: delegator ID
delegator_id.description: Identifies the actor issuing the delegation and bearing authorization responsibility.

delegatee_id.title: delegatee ID
delegatee_id.description: Identifies the actor accepting the delegation and responsible for performance.

task_id.title: task ID
task_id.description: Pins the task covered by this delegation.

budget_id.title: budget ID
budget_id.description: Identifies the time, cost, or tool budget limiting delegated performance.

DelegationEvent.title: delegation event payload
DelegationEvent.description: Delegation event is an event whose defining condition is that it is an observable event that initiates, updates, cancels, or completes delegated work under a delegation contract.

delegation_event_id.title: event ID
delegation_event_id.description: Records the event ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

delegation_process_id.title: delegation-process ID
delegation_process_id.description: Records the delegation-process ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

event_kind.title: event kind
event_kind.description: Records the event kind needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: Records the occurred at needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

sequence_index.title: sequence index
sequence_index.description: Records the sequence index needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Records the actor ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

supersedes_event_id.title: superseded event ID
supersedes_event_id.description: Records the superseded event ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation event instance.

DelegationOwnership.title: delegation ownership payload
DelegationOwnership.description: Delegation ownership is a coordination ownership record; its differentiating condition is that it records the responsibility split between delegator and delegatee, including retained answer ownership, transferred control, authority scope, and result accountability.

ownership_record_id.title: ownership-record ID
ownership_record_id.description: Records the ownership-record ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

delegation_id.title: delegation ID
delegation_id.description: Records the delegation ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

delegator_id.title: delegator ID
delegator_id.description: Records the delegator ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

delegatee_id.title: delegatee ID
delegatee_id.description: Records the delegatee ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

control_owner_id.title: control owner
control_owner_id.description: Records the control owner needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

answer_owner_id.title: answer owner
answer_owner_id.description: Records the answer owner needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

result_accountable_actor_id.title: result-accountable actor
result_accountable_actor_id.description: Records the result-accountable actor needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

responsibility_scope.title: responsibility scope
responsibility_scope.description: Records the responsibility scope needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

effective_at.title: effective at
effective_at.description: Records the effective at needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

return_condition.title: return condition
return_condition.description: Records the return condition needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation ownership instance.

DelegationPhase.title: Delegation phase payload
DelegationPhase.description: Delegation phase belongs to the state category and differs from broader members in that it is a lifecycle state of a delegation process that is distinguishable by responsibility, authority, and completion conditions.

DelegationProcess.title: delegation process payload
DelegationProcess.description: Delegation process is a Collaboration process; its differentiating condition is that it is a coordination activity in which a delegator assigns scoped work, authority, context, and budget under a contract to a delegate role and receives a traceable result with explicit retained or transferred ownership.

DelegationResult.title: delegation result payload
DelegationResult.description: Delegation result is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable result returned by a worker, subagent, tool-like agent, or remote delegate, including artifacts, status, warnings, and provenance.

delegation_result_id.title: result ID
delegation_result_id.description: Records the result ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

delegation_process_id.title: delegation-process ID
delegation_process_id.description: Records the delegation-process ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

status.title: result status
status.description: Records the result status needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

returned_at.title: returned at
returned_at.description: Records the returned at needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

artifact_refs.title: artifact references
artifact_refs.description: Records the artifact references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

warning_refs.title: warning references
warning_refs.description: Records the warning references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

provenance_ref.title: provenance reference
provenance_ref.description: Records the provenance reference needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation result instance.

Handoff.title: handoff payload
Handoff.description: Handoff is an event whose defining condition is that it is a control-transfer event that moves responsibility for a task, conversation, route, or runtime state to another agent actor or remote delegate.

handoff_event_id.title: handoff-event ID
handoff_event_id.description: Records the handoff-event ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

handoff_process_id.title: handoff-process ID
handoff_process_id.description: Records the handoff-process ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: Records the occurred at needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

source_actor_id.title: source actor
source_actor_id.description: Records the source actor needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

target_actor_id.title: target actor
target_actor_id.description: Records the target actor needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

responsibility_scope.title: responsibility scope
responsibility_scope.description: Records the responsibility scope needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

context_id.title: context ID
context_id.description: Records the context ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a handoff instance.

HandoffProcess.title: Handoff process payload
HandoffProcess.description: A handoff process is a collaboration process that transfers control, responsibility, and required context for ongoing work between accountable parties; a Handoff event is only an observable point in that process.

HandoffTarget.title: handoff target payload
HandoffTarget.description: Handoff target belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is an actor, remote agent, route, or protocol endpoint that receives control or responsibility after a handoff.

InitiationPhase.title: Initiation phase payload
InitiationPhase.description: Initiation phase belongs to the Delegation phase category and differs from broader members in that the delegator proposes scope, authority, and acceptance conditions before the delegate accepts.

ResponsibilityTransfer.title: responsibility transfer payload
ResponsibilityTransfer.description: Responsibility transfer belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records which responsibility changes owner during delegation or handoff, including task custody, control custody, answer ownership, and audit accountability.

responsibility_transfer_id.title: transfer-record ID
responsibility_transfer_id.description: Records the transfer-record ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

handoff_event_id.title: handoff-event ID
handoff_event_id.description: Records the handoff-event ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

responsibility_scope.title: responsibility scope
responsibility_scope.description: Records the responsibility scope needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

previous_owner_id.title: previous owner
previous_owner_id.description: Records the previous owner needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

new_owner_id.title: new owner
new_owner_id.description: Records the new owner needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

effective_at.title: effective at
effective_at.description: Records the effective at needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

retained_scope.title: retained scope
retained_scope.description: Records the retained scope needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

excluded_scope.title: excluded scope
excluded_scope.description: Records the excluded scope needed to identify, validate, or replay a responsibility transfer instance.

RevocationPhase.title: Revocation phase payload
RevocationPhase.description: Revocation phase is a Delegation phase; its differentiating condition is that the delegation is withdrawn, invalidated, or terminated before completion and subsequent control must be reassigned.

SubagentContext.title: Subagent context payload
SubagentContext.description: Subagent context is delegation context specialized for a subagent recipient role, containing the task state, messages, memory, tools, and sources required and authorized for its delegated work.

context_id.title: context ID
context_id.description: Records the context ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

version.title: version
version.description: Records the version needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

delegation_id.title: delegation ID
delegation_id.description: Records the delegation ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

content_digest.title: content digest
content_digest.description: Records the content digest needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

allowed_reference_ids.title: allowed references
allowed_reference_ids.description: Records the allowed references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

excluded_reference_ids.title: excluded references
excluded_reference_ids.description: Records the excluded references needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: Records the created at needed to identify, validate, or replay a delegation context instance.

TaskDistribution.title: task distribution payload
TaskDistribution.description: Task distribution is a Worker assignment activity; its differentiating condition is that it is a DelegationProcess distinguished by assigning work items to selected performers with scope evidence.

WorkerAssignment.title: Worker assignment activity payload
WorkerAssignment.description: Worker assignment activity is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it binds work items, responsibility, authority, and acceptance conditions to the selected worker role.

WorkerAvailability.title: worker availability payload
WorkerAvailability.description: Worker availability belongs to the state category and differs from broader members in that it records whether a worker or subagent can accept work, including load, lifecycle state, capability readiness, and fallback status.

availability_assessment_id.title: availability-assessment ID
availability_assessment_id.description: Records the availability-assessment ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

worker_role_id.title: worker-role ID
worker_role_id.description: Records the worker-role ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

availability_status.title: availability status
availability_status.description: Records the availability status needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

load_ratio.title: load ratio
load_ratio.description: Records the load ratio needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

assessed_at.title: assessed at
assessed_at.description: Records the assessed at needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Records the valid until needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

reason.title: reason
reason.description: Records the reason needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker availability instance.

WorkerAvailabilityAssessment.title: Worker availability assessment payload
WorkerAvailabilityAssessment.description: Worker availability assessment is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it checks whether candidate roles can accept work within required time, budget, and concurrency bounds.

WorkerCapabilityMatch.title: worker capability-match evidence payload
WorkerCapabilityMatch.description: Worker capability-match evidence belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records the evidence that a worker, subagent, model, or tool matches the required task capability, modality, authority, or domain.

matching_activity_id.title: matching-activity ID
matching_activity_id.description: Records the matching-activity ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

worker_role_id.title: worker-role ID
worker_role_id.description: Records the worker-role ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

requirement_ref.title: requirement reference
requirement_ref.description: Records the requirement reference needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

score.title: match score
score.description: Records the match score needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

matched.title: matched
matched.description: Records the matched needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

reason.title: match reason
reason.description: Records the match reason needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

evidence_refs.title: evidence references
evidence_refs.description: Records the evidence references needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

assessed_at.title: assessed at
assessed_at.description: Records the assessed at needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker capability match instance.

WorkerCapabilityMatching.title: Worker capability matching payload
WorkerCapabilityMatching.description: Worker capability matching is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it compares task requirements and authority with candidate-worker capability evidence.

WorkerPool.title: worker pool payload
WorkerPool.description: As a collection, Worker pool is distinguished by the fact that it is a candidate set of workers, subagents, models, tools, or remote delegates available for selection by capability, load, cost, authority, and trust boundary.

worker_pool_id.title: worker-pool ID
worker_pool_id.description: Records the worker-pool ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker pool instance.

version.title: version
version.description: Records the version needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker pool instance.

delegation_id.title: delegation ID
delegation_id.description: Records the delegation ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker pool instance.

selection_scope.title: selection scope
selection_scope.description: Records the selection scope needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker pool instance.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: Records the created at needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker pool instance.

WorkerSelection.title: worker selection payload
WorkerSelection.description: As an event, Worker selection is distinguished by the fact that it is a decision event that chooses a worker, subagent, model, tool, or route from a candidate pool using capability, availability, cost, safety, and context-fit evidence.

selection_id.title: selection ID
selection_id.description: Records the selection ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

decision_activity_id.title: decision-activity ID
decision_activity_id.description: Records the decision-activity ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

worker_pool_id.title: worker-pool ID
worker_pool_id.description: Records the worker-pool ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

selected_worker_role_id.title: selected worker-role ID
selected_worker_role_id.description: Records the selected worker-role ID needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: Records the decided at needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Records the selection reason needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

rejected_candidate_reasons.title: rejected-candidate reasons
rejected_candidate_reasons.description: Records the rejected-candidate reasons needed to identify, validate, or replay a worker selection instance.

WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity.title: Worker selection decision activity payload
WorkerSelectionDecisionActivity.description: Worker selection decision activity is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it chooses a delegate among available candidates using match evidence and the delegation contract.

WorkerSelectionProcess.title: Worker selection process payload
WorkerSelectionProcess.description: Worker selection process is a delegation process; its differentiating condition is that it is a collaboration process within delegation that assesses availability, matches capability, makes a selection, and assigns work.

EvaluationAttempt.title: Evaluation attempt payload
EvaluationAttempt.description: An evaluation attempt is an improvement attempt coordinating one feedback-owned EvaluationRun within an improvement loop.

EvaluatorOptimizer.title: evaluator optimizer payload
EvaluatorOptimizer.description: Evaluator optimizer is a role whose defining condition is that EvaluatorOptimizer denotes responsibility for reviewing output and choosing revision, retry, escalation, or stop; it is a role, not a loop or fixed actor subtype.

EvaluatorOptimizerLoop.title: Evaluator-optimizer loop payload
EvaluatorOptimizerLoop.description: As an improvement loop, Evaluator-optimizer loop is distinguished by the fact that it alternates evaluation and revision attempts until a stop condition holds.

loop_id.title: improvement-loop ID
loop_id.description: Stable identifier for one bounded review, revision, and retry loop.

maximum_attempts.title: maximum attempts
maximum_attempts.description: Maximum attempts the loop may contain before forced stopping.

status.title: loop status
status.description: Whether the loop is active, succeeded, failed, budget-stopped, or human-stopped.

evaluated_subject_id.title: evaluated-subject ID
evaluated_subject_id.description: Pins the subject version being improved by the loop.

stop_reason.title: stop reason
stop_reason.description: Success, failure, budget, safety, or human reason recorded when the loop terminates.

FeedbackRouting.title: feedback routing payload
FeedbackRouting.description: Feedback routing belongs to the review coordination category and differs from broader members in that it reads ReviewFinding, FeedbackEvent, and applicable policy, selects a task, worker, revision plan, gate, or optimization loop as destination, and routes Feedback; the activity has execution identity, while its input information records do not.

ImprovementAttempt.title: improvement attempt payload
ImprovementAttempt.description: Improvement attempt belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is a bounded retry or revision attempt launched from feedback, review, gate outcome, or stop-retry lineage.

ImprovementLoop.title: improvement loop payload
ImprovementLoop.description: As an activity, Improvement loop is distinguished by the fact that it is a bounded control activity that receives an evaluated target and review or feedback evidence, forms revision plans, and produces one or more new attempts until success, failure, budget, safety, or human stop conditions hold.

loop_id.title: improvement-loop ID
loop_id.description: Stable identifier for one bounded review, revision, and retry loop.

maximum_attempts.title: maximum attempts
maximum_attempts.description: Maximum attempts the loop may contain before forced stopping.

status.title: loop status
status.description: Whether the loop is active, succeeded, failed, budget-stopped, or human-stopped.

evaluated_subject_id.title: evaluated-subject ID
evaluated_subject_id.description: Pins the subject version being improved by the loop.

stop_reason.title: stop reason
stop_reason.description: Success, failure, budget, safety, or human reason recorded when the loop terminates.

OptimizerState.title: optimizer state payload
OptimizerState.description: As a state, Optimizer state is distinguished by the fact that it is a runtime-visible state of an evaluator-optimizer loop, including current attempt, selected feedback, budget, stopping context, and candidate quality.

ReviewAssignment.title: review assignment payload
ReviewAssignment.description: Review assignment is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it assigns review responsibility to a human, model, evaluator, policy gate, or worker and identifies the artifact or decision under review.

ReviewCoordination.title: review coordination payload
ReviewCoordination.description: Review coordination is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it organizes review responsibility, initiates review events, collects critique or feedback, and routes applicable results to tasks, roles, revision plans, gates, or improvement loops.

ReviewRevisionLoop.title: Review-revision loop payload
ReviewRevisionLoop.description: Review-revision loop is an improvement loop; its differentiating condition is that it schedules revision and reevaluation using review coordination, findings, and correction evidence.

loop_id.title: improvement-loop ID
loop_id.description: Stable identifier for one bounded review, revision, and retry loop.

maximum_attempts.title: maximum attempts
maximum_attempts.description: Maximum attempts the loop may contain before forced stopping.

status.title: loop status
status.description: Whether the loop is active, succeeded, failed, budget-stopped, or human-stopped.

evaluated_subject_id.title: evaluated-subject ID
evaluated_subject_id.description: Pins the subject version being improved by the loop.

stop_reason.title: stop reason
stop_reason.description: Success, failure, budget, safety, or human reason recorded when the loop terminates.

RevisionAttempt.title: Revision attempt payload
RevisionAttempt.description: A revision attempt is an improvement attempt coordinating correction activity, a revision plan, and subsequent validation within an improvement loop.

StopRetryLineage.title: stop retry lineage payload
StopRetryLineage.description: Stop retry lineage belongs to the revision artifact category and differs from broader members in that it links a review, failure, or gate outcome to the bounded retry, revision, stop, or rollback path chosen for an orchestration loop.

ThinkAsTool.title: think as tool payload
ThinkAsTool.description: As an activity, Think as tool is distinguished by the fact that it is a visible deliberation or review operation exposed as a bounded tool-like step with explicit inputs, outputs, and audit metadata.

BranchCondition.title: branch condition payload
BranchCondition.description: Branch condition belongs to the routing condition category and differs from broader members in that it is a condition over task state, trace evidence, policy, feedback, or capability match that determines which branch may run next.

ControlGate.title: control gate payload
ControlGate.description: A ControlGate is a capability at an orchestration control point that evaluates conditions and evidence, produces an auditable outcome, and allows, blocks, redirects, retries, escalates, or stops downstream work.

DownstreamOperation.title: downstream operation payload
DownstreamOperation.description: Downstream operation belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is reference information for an executable branch, tool, protocol, or stop path; it is not a Route or Gate subtype.

Gate.title: gate payload
Gate.description: Gate belongs to the control gate category and differs from broader members in that it is a Controlit capability that allows, blocks, redirects, or escalates work based on conditions.

GateCondition.title: gate condition payload
GateCondition.description: As a routing condition, Gate condition is distinguished by the fact that it is a condition checked at a workflow gate to decide whether control may proceed, block, redirect, escalate, retry, or stop.

GateOutcome.title: gate outcome payload
GateOutcome.description: Gate outcome belongs to the state category and differs from broader members in that it is the observable allowed, blocked, redirected, or escalated state produced by evaluation, not a kind of gate.

RetryPolicy.title: retry policy payload
RetryPolicy.description: Retry policy is a routing specification whose defining condition is that it is a policy bounding when an orchestration loop may retry work, including retry count, changed context, backoff, escalation, and failure conditions.

Route.title: route payload
Route.description: Route is a routing specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a RoutingSpecification declaring control flow to a typed target; the actual selection occurs in RoutingDecision.

RoutingCondition.title: routing condition payload
RoutingCondition.description: Routing condition is a routing specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a RoutingSpecification evaluable from task state, trace evidence, policy, budget, safety, or human decision to enable a branch, pass a gate, or terminate flow.

RoutingDecision.title: routing decision payload
RoutingDecision.description: Routing decision is an event whose defining condition is that it is an observable decision event that applies routing policy to select a routing target and record why that target was chosen.

decision_id.title: routing-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one replayable routing choice.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the routing choice took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains how evaluated conditions and policy support the selected target.

RoutingPolicy.title: routing policy payload
RoutingPolicy.description: As a routing specification, Routing policy is distinguished by the fact that it is a policy used by orchestration to choose a branch, worker, tool, protocol, retry, escalation, or stop path from current state and evidence.

RoutingSpecification.title: routing specification payload
RoutingSpecification.description: Routing specification belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is normative information describing eligible orchestration paths, selection policy, applicable conditions, retry bounds, or termination conditions that a routing decision can apply.

RoutingTarget.title: routing target payload
RoutingTarget.description: Routing target is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is a typed endpoint selected by a route, such as a worker, subagent, tool, branch, protocol adapter, operation handle, or stop path.

StopCondition.title: stop condition payload
StopCondition.description: Stop condition belongs to the routing condition category and differs from broader members in that it is a condition under which an orchestration loop must stop because success, failure, budget exhaustion, safety, or human decision has been reached.

Goal.title: goal payload
Goal.description: Goal is an intentional entity; its differentiating condition is that it is a high-level user or system intent that motivates an agent workflow before it is decomposed into operational objectives, tasks, or success criteria.

Intent.title: intent payload
Intent.description: Intent is an intentional entity whose defining condition is that it is an itionalEntity adopted by a named actor that expresses commitment to act toward a goal within defined scope and constraints; it is not the goal, plan, or execution itself.

IntentionalEntity.title: intentional entity payload
IntentionalEntity.description: Intentional entity is a specification whose defining condition is that it is normative planning information expressing a desired future state, commitment to act, or measurable target that can be refined into work specifications.

Objective.title: objective payload
Objective.description: Objective is an intentional entity; its differentiating condition is that it is an operational and measurable target derived from a goal that can be assigned, checked, and traced through tasks, constraints, and completion criteria.

Task.title: task payload
Task.description: A Task is a WorkSpecification identified by task_id that describes assignable work through status, priority, constraints, and completion criteria; one concrete execution is represented by a RunAttempt.

task_id.title: task ID
task_id.description: Stable identifier for the task across its planning and execution lineage.

status.title: task status
status.description: Current state of the task in its controlled workflow lifecycle.

priority.title: priority
priority.description: Explicit scheduling priority; the project policy defines its numeric ordering.

constraint_summary.title: constraint summary
constraint_summary.description: Human-readable summary of the principal constraints governing task execution.

TaskCompletionCriterion.title: task completion criterion payload
TaskCompletionCriterion.description: As a task condition, Task completion criterion is distinguished by the fact that it specifies testable conditions, thresholds, and evidence required for a task to be complete; EvaluationRun applies it rather than the criterion evaluating a run outcome itself.

TaskCondition.title: task condition payload
TaskCondition.description: Task condition is a specification whose defining condition is that it is an evaluable condition specification used by planning or control logic to govern whether a task may start, how it executes, or when it is complete, including criteria, constraints, and dependencies.

TaskConstraint.title: task constraint payload
TaskConstraint.description: As a task condition, Task constraint is distinguished by the fact that it records a planning constraint such as deadline, budget, authority, capability, context, safety, or dependency limit that shapes task execution.

TaskDependency.title: task dependency payload
TaskDependency.description: Task dependency is a task condition whose defining condition is that it states that one task, step, or work item depends on another result, condition, resource, or approval before it can proceed.

TaskPlan.title: task plan payload
TaskPlan.description: Task plan is a work specification whose defining condition is that it organizes a goal into ordered tasks, work items, dependencies, constraints, and completion criteria that can be delegated and audited.

plan_id.title: plan ID
plan_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical task plan across revisions.

version.title: plan version
version.description: Identifies one immutable revision of the task plan.

status.title: plan status
status.description: Controlled state such as drafted, reviewed, approved, executing, or closed.

created_at.title: created at
created_at.description: UTC instant at which this plan version was created.

TaskStep.title: task step payload
TaskStep.description: Task step is a work specification; its differentiating condition is that it specifies ordering, dependencies, owner, completion evidence, and an optional invocation plan; it does not execute a tool call, whose execution identity belongs to ToolCall and ToolCallAttempt.

WorkItem.title: work item payload
WorkItem.description: Work item is a work specification whose defining condition is that it is an assignable unit of planned work derived from a task and scoped to an actor, tool, subagent, route, or artifact outcome.

WorkSpecification.title: work specification payload
WorkSpecification.description: As a specification, Work specification is distinguished by the fact that it describes a unit or structure of work and specifies scope, performer requirements, inputs, expected outputs, and completion evidence, separately from actual execution activity.

Actor.title: runtime actor payload
Actor.description: A runtime Actor is an entity identifiable in traces that can initiate or receive actions and bear roles, capabilities, authority, and accountability during agent-system operation.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

ActorAuthorityScope.title: actor authority scope payload
ActorAuthorityScope.description: authority-scope record that bounds the operations, resources, tools, data zones, and trust boundaries a runtime actor may access.

ActorBinding.title: actor binding payload
ActorBinding.description: As an information record, Actor binding is distinguished by the fact that it is information that links one runtime actor, for a defined scope and validity period, to a borne role, available capability, or granted authority and records grantor, conditions, and provenance.

ActorCapabilityBinding.title: actor capability binding payload
ActorCapabilityBinding.description: Actor capability binding is an actor binding whose defining condition is that it is a binding record that connects a runtime actor to callable, observable, retrieval, generation, or review capabilities together with allowed conditions of use.

ActorRoleBinding.title: actor role binding payload
ActorRoleBinding.description: Actor role binding is an actor binding whose defining condition is that it is an assignment record that binds a runtime actor to a responsibility-bearing role so delegation, review, approval, and accountability remain traceable.

AgentActor.title: agent actor payload
AgentActor.description: As a software actor, Agent actor is distinguished by the fact that it is a runtime identity capable of acting, observing, invoking tools, receiving delegation, emitting trace events, and being accountable for session activity.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

DeveloperActor.title: developer actor payload
DeveloperActor.description: Developer actor is a human actor; its differentiating condition is that it is a runtime participant responsible for developer-level instructions, implementation constraints, environment setup, or review decisions.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

EmbeddingModel.title: embedding model payload
EmbeddingModel.description: As a runtime model, Embedding model is distinguished by the fact that it is a Model distinguished by producing representations for retrieval or matching.

ExternalServiceActor.title: external service actor payload
ExternalServiceActor.description: External service actor belongs to the software actor category and differs from broader members in that it is a runtime participant outside the controlled system that can receive requests, return results, or introduce trust-boundary and provenance obligations.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

GenerativeModel.title: generative model payload
GenerativeModel.description: Generative model is a runtime model; its differentiating condition is that it is a Model distinguished by generating text, code, structured output, or call arguments.

HumanActor.title: human actor payload
HumanActor.description: A HumanActor is a runtime Actor borne by a natural person who can request, operate, review, approve, or correct system work.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

HumanOperator.title: human operator payload
HumanOperator.description: Human operator belongs to the human actor category and differs from broader members in that it is a person who can approve, pause, resume, redirect, review, or supply missing information during a runtime session.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

Model.title: runtime model payload
Model.description: Runtime model belongs to the entity category and differs from broader members in that it is a version-identifiable model entity with capability boundaries, invoked by software actors to generate, embed, rank, evaluate, or classify information.

ModelActor.title: model actor payload
ModelActor.description: Model actor is a software actor whose defining condition is that it is a model-backed runtime participant that generates, embeds, ranks, evaluates, transforms, or classifies information while producing traceable spans and budget use.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Stable identifier for the subject that bears actions or responsibilities within the system boundary.

actor_kind.title: actor kind
actor_kind.description: Controlled category distinguishing human, software, or external-service actors.

display_name.title: display name
display_name.description: Human-readable name for audit displays that does not determine identity.

RemoteAgentReference.title: remote agent reference payload
RemoteAgentReference.description: Remote agent reference is an information record whose defining condition is that it is reference information pointing to a remote actor; a reference is not an AgentActor and does not assert ownership of remote internals.

RerankerModel.title: reranker model payload
RerankerModel.description: Reranker model is a runtime model; its differentiating condition is that it is a Model distinguished by reordering candidate items using relevance evidence.

ReviewerActor.title: reviewer actor payload

UserActor.title: user actor payload
UserActor.description: As a human actor, User actor is distinguished by the fact that it is a human or client-side participant whose requests, feedback, approvals, corrections, or attachments enter the runtime as observable inputs.

Artifact.description: Artifact is an information record whose defining condition is that it is a runtime work product with provenance, produced or consumed by attempts, events, actors, tools, or sessions and traceable through derivation, attribution, review, and export.
