Execution.title: Execution payload
Execution.description: Execution is the activity in which a participant performs work in a runtime environment under a plan or invocation specification and produces an observable outcome.

RetryActivity.title: Retry activity payload
RetryActivity.description: Retry activity is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it creates a new run attempt under an applicable retry policy and predecessor evidence while retaining lineage, attempt number, and budget.

RunAttempt.title: run attempt payload
RunAttempt.description: As an Execution, Run attempt is distinguished by the fact that it is a bounded activity with retry lineage; it belongs to a session but is not a subtype of RuntimeSession.

status.title: Execution status
status.description: Records the controlled lifecycle state of one Run Attempt.

duration_ms.title: Duration milliseconds
duration_ms.description: captures duration in milliseconds as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

retry_count.title: Retry count
retry_count.description: captures retry count as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

legacy_status_text.title: Legacy status text
legacy_status_text.description: captures status as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

attempt_number.title: attempt number
attempt_number.description: A one-based, monotonically increasing attempt number within one Execution.

retry_of.title: retry of
retry_of.description: If this attempt is a retry, references its immediate predecessor; empty for the initial attempt.

RunOutcome.title: Run outcome payload
RunOutcome.description: Run outcome belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it expresses only whether one run attempt ultimately succeeded, failed, or was cancelled; it does not carry content, citations, logs, or tool return values produced by the attempt.

run_attempt_id.title: run attempt ID
run_attempt_id.description: Uniquely identifies the run attempt to which this outcome belongs.

outcome_code.title: outcome code
outcome_code.description: The controlled terminal disposition of one execution attempt: succeeded, failed, or cancelled.

RunResult.title: Run result record payload
RunResult.description: As an information record, Run result record is distinguished by the fact that it is addressable information containing the content, citations, artifact pointers, tool return values, and diagnostic evidence actually produced by one run attempt; it is separate from the terminal succeeded, failed, or cancelled disposition.

result_id.title: result record ID
result_id.description: Uniquely identifies one run result record.

run_attempt_id.title: run attempt ID
run_attempt_id.description: Identifies the run attempt that produced this record.

RuntimeBudget.title: runtime budget payload
RuntimeBudget.description: Runtime budget is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a runtime limit record for time, token, cost, retry, tool-call, context-window, or resource consumption constraints applied to an execution attempt or session.

cost.title: Cost
cost.description: captures cost as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

AgentTranscript.title: agent transcript payload
AgentTranscript.description: Agent transcript belongs to the observability record category and differs from broader members in that it is an ordered runtime transcript of messages, actions, tool calls, model outputs, policy decisions, warnings, and artifact references that were actually visible or emitted.

AuditRecord.title: audit record payload
AuditRecord.description: Audit record is an observability record whose defining condition is that it is a raw audit evidence attached to a runtime session or trace, preserving what happened and who or what was associated without performing feedback interpretation.

Checkpoint.title: checkpoint payload
Checkpoint.description: A Checkpoint is a StateRecord that stores session, version, creating activity, state digest, and referenced StateSnapshot; CheckpointActivity performs capture, while the record itself neither mutates nor generates state.

CheckpointActivity.title: Checkpoint activity payload
CheckpointActivity.description: Checkpoint activity belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it creates, restores, or replays a verifiable state boundary in a runtime session; the activity and its Checkpoint, Snapshot, Diff, and event records retain separate identities.

CheckpointRestoreEvent.title: checkpoint restore event payload
CheckpointRestoreEvent.description: Checkpoint restore event is an observability event whose defining condition is that it is an observable event that restores a runtime session, actor, graph, or workflow from a saved checkpoint and records restore provenance.

CommandExecution.title: command execution payload
CommandExecution.description: Command execution is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it is a runtime activity observed by trace records; an activity is not a subtype of a record, span, or recorded event.

ObservabilityEvent.title: observability event payload
ObservabilityEvent.description: An ObservabilityEvent is an event at a determinate time that changes or reports runtime state and can be correlated to a trace, session, or attempt; the occurrence is distinct from the record carrying it.

ObservabilityRecord.title: observability record payload
ObservabilityRecord.description: Observability record is an information record whose defining condition is that it is time- and provenance-bearing information that preserves runtime behavior, state, messages, or accountability evidence with correlation identifiers, excluding unexternalized private reasoning.

ObservableSummary.title: observable summary payload
ObservableSummary.description: Observable summary is an observability record; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable runtime summary that compresses prior events, state, transcript, or artifact evidence for context budget, review, or handoff without storing private reasoning transcripts.

ReplayActivity.title: Replay activity payload
ReplayActivity.description: Replay activity is a Checkpoint activity whose defining condition is that it re-executes a trace or checkpoint interval under recorded ordering and inputs to verify behavior or locate divergence.

ReplayEvent.title: replay event payload
ReplayEvent.description: Replay event is an observability event; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable event that replays a trace, span, session segment, checkpoint, or artifact lineage for debugging, evaluation, reproduction, or review.

RestoreActivity.title: Restore activity payload
RestoreActivity.description: Restore activity belongs to the Checkpoint activity category and differs from broader members in that it reconstructs a successor state snapshot from one checkpoint and records the before/after difference.

SpanAttribute.title: span attribute payload
SpanAttribute.description: Span attribute is a trace metadata whose defining condition is that it is a keyed attribute attached to a trace span to record operation name, model/tool identity, status detail, latency, token use, or other observable metadata.

SpanStatus.title: span status payload
SpanStatus.description: Span status is a trace metadata whose defining condition is that it is a status record for a trace span, such as unset, ok, error, cancelled, blocked, retried, or degraded, with optional diagnostic context.

StateDiff.title: state diff payload
StateDiff.description: State diff is a state record whose defining condition is that it is a difference record from one state snapshot to another, showing changed values, channels, artifacts, or lifecycle positions between two captured states.

StateRecord.title: state record payload
StateRecord.description: As an observability record, State record is distinguished by the fact that it is an ObservabilityRecord whose primary content is runtime state at a version or time, a difference between versions, or a recoverable consistency boundary.

StateSnapshot.title: state snapshot payload
StateSnapshot.description: State snapshot belongs to the state record category and differs from broader members in that it is a captured runtime state for a session, actor, graph, tool, or statechart step, used for inspection, recovery, replay, or lifecycle transition.

TelemetryEvent.title: telemetry event payload
TelemetryEvent.description: Telemetry event belongs to the event category and differs from broader members in that it is an instrumented signal derived from runtime trace evidence and emitted for collection, monitoring, diagnostics, metrics, or export without redefining the raw TraceEvent.

TraceContext.title: trace context payload
TraceContext.description: As a trace metadata, Trace context is distinguished by the fact that it is a correlation context carrying trace identifiers, parent context, sampling state, propagation metadata, and boundary information for a trace record.

TraceEvent.title: trace event payload
TraceEvent.description: Trace event belongs to the observability event category and differs from broader members in that it is an observable runtime occurrence such as a message, action, tool call, state update, warning, policy decision, artifact update, or checkpoint event.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: captures timestamp as a date-time scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

TraceLink.title: trace link payload
TraceLink.description: Trace link belongs to the trace metadata category and differs from broader members in that it is a non-parent causal or contextual association between spans, events, traces, attempts, or artifacts when ordinary parent-child trace structure is insufficient.

TraceMetadata.title: trace metadata payload
TraceMetadata.description: As an information record, Trace metadata is distinguished by the fact that it is information attached to a trace or span to propagate context, express non-parental links, or record attributes and status without representing the observed activity itself.

TraceRecord.title: trace record payload
TraceRecord.description: Trace record is an observability record whose defining condition is that it is a trace container for one runtime workflow or session segment, grouping spans, trace context, links, ordered events, retention policy, and root-span identity.

trace_id.title: trace ID
trace_id.description: Stable identifier correlating one end-to-end runtime trace across spans.

sampled.title: sampled
sampled.description: Whether this trace was retained by the applicable sampling decision.

TraceRetentionPolicy.title: trace retention policy payload
TraceRetentionPolicy.description: Trace retention policy is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a policy governing trace storage duration, sampling, redaction, visibility, export destination, deletion, privacy class, and no-retention modes.

TraceSpan.title: trace span payload
TraceSpan.description: Trace span belongs to the observability record category and differs from broader members in that it is an operation interval or unit of work inside a trace, with parent span, timing, status, attributes, contained events, and links to related spans.

AgentSystem.title: agent system payload
AgentSystem.description: As an entity, Agent system is distinguished by the fact that it is a runtime arrangement that coordinates actors, models, tools, memory, policies, traces, sessions, and artifacts to perform bounded agent work.

system_id.title: System ID
system_id.description: captures identifier as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

Container.title: container payload
Container.description: Container is a runtime environment; its differentiating condition is that it describes a runtime execution environment with filesystem state, process isolation, network settings, and session-local resources.

environment_variables.title: environment variable
environment_variables.description: A name-value binding is a repeatable structural value of RuntimeEnvironment without independent business identity; keeping it as a node would turn a schema field into a concept.

ProcessHandle.title: process handle payload
ProcessHandle.description: Process handle is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it identifies the operating-system process reference used to monitor, stop, or correlate an execution attempt.

RuntimeEnvironment.title: runtime environment payload
RuntimeEnvironment.description: Runtime environment belongs to the entity category and differs from broader members in that it describes the concrete execution environment for a session, including container, sandbox, working directory, process settings, model/tool availability, and runtime configuration.

environment_variables.title: environment variable
environment_variables.description: A name-value binding is a repeatable structural value of RuntimeEnvironment without independent business identity; keeping it as a node would turn a schema field into a concept.

RuntimeSession.title: runtime session payload
RuntimeSession.description: As an activity, Runtime session is distinguished by the fact that it is a bounded execution episode grouping attempts, budget, environment, and artifacts under one identity; those items enter through composition relations.

lifecycle_state.title: lifecycle state
lifecycle_state.description: Current state in the controlled session lifecycle, not a separate graph node.

end_time.title: end time
end_time.description: UTC instant at which the session-end event took effect; null while active.

start_time.title: start time
start_time.description: UTC instant at which the session-start event took effect.

session_id.title: session ID
session_id.description: Stable identifier for this runtime session within one system deployment.

SessionEndEvent.title: session end event payload
SessionEndEvent.description: Session end event belongs to the session lifecycle event category and differs from broader members in that it records the observable closure of a runtime session, including final status, cleanup, artifacts, and remaining audit references.

SessionLifecycleEvent.title: session lifecycle event payload
SessionLifecycleEvent.description: A SessionLifecycleEvent is an event at a determinate time that enters, exits, or restores a runtime-session lifecycle state while recording prior state, resulting state, actor, cause, and trace references.

SessionPauseEvent.title: session pause event payload
SessionPauseEvent.description: As a session lifecycle event, Session pause event is distinguished by the fact that it records an observable transition where a session stops progressing while preserving resumable state and pending work.

SessionResumeEvent.title: session resume event payload
SessionResumeEvent.description: Session resume event is a session lifecycle event; its differentiating condition is that it records the observable transition that restores a paused session from retained state, checkpoint, or external continuation.

SessionStartEvent.title: session start event payload
SessionStartEvent.description: As a session lifecycle event, Session start event is distinguished by the fact that it records the observable event that opens a runtime session and binds the session to initial actor, environment, goal, and context state.

WorkingDirectory.title: working directory payload
WorkingDirectory.description: Working directory is an information record whose defining condition is that it records the filesystem location that scopes relative paths and process execution for a runtime or tool invocation.

AgencyBenchAdapter.title: AgencyBench adapter payload
AgencyBenchAdapter.description: As a benchmark adapter, AgencyBench adapter is distinguished by the fact that it maps AgencyBench long-horizon tasks, tool-call pressure, context length, multi-step observations, and scoring evidence into canonical task, runtime, tool, and evaluation adapter terms.

It is an aggregate report and does not replace individual rules or test results.

MappingArtifact.title: mapping artifact payload
MappingArtifact.description: Mapping artifact is an information record whose defining condition is that it is an information object with identifier, version, author or producer, applicable mapping, and evidence provenance. These artifacts may appear in Adapter node details while retaining their own identity and provenance and do not form a second graph.

Candidate.title: Candidate Record payload
Candidate.description: A Candidate Record is an information record that represents an object considered under a particular query and discovery activity, retaining its reference, source, and preliminary applicability; candidacy does not imply selection or execution authorization.

CapabilityCandidate.title: capability candidate payload
CapabilityCandidate.description: Capability candidate is a Candidate Record; its differentiating condition is that it represents a possible tool, resource, prompt, API operation, or hosted capability under consideration during discovery and selection.

CapabilitySelectionDecision.title: capability selection decision payload
CapabilitySelectionDecision.description: Capability selection decision is a Selection Decision whose defining condition is that it is a selection event that chooses, rejects, or defers a capability candidate across tools, resources, prompts, and API operations.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

DiscoveryCandidateSet.title: Discovery Candidate Set payload
DiscoveryCandidateSet.description: Discovery Candidate Set belongs to the collection category and differs from broader members in that it records from the same query and discovery batch and records completeness, pre-ranking state, and provenance; it does not denote memory-retrieval candidate sets, and rankings or selection decisions are not member types.

MatchAssessment.title: Match Assessment payload
MatchAssessment.description: Match Assessment is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it records a candidate's fit to a query, requirement, or constraint, including method, value, threshold, and provenance; it supports but is not the selection decision.

PromptCandidate.title: prompt candidate payload
PromptCandidate.description: Prompt candidate is a Candidate Record; its differentiating condition is that it is returned by discovery, filtering, or protocol listing before a prompt selection decision chooses whether it may be retrieved or instantiated.

PromptSelectionDecision.title: prompt selection decision payload
PromptSelectionDecision.description: Prompt selection decision is a Selection Decision whose defining condition is that it is a selection event that chooses, rejects, or defers a prompt candidate based on task fit, argument availability, authority, and workflow intent.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

ResourceCandidate.title: resource candidate payload
ResourceCandidate.description: Resource candidate belongs to the Candidate Record category and differs from broader members in that it is returned by discovery, filtering, or protocol listing before a resource selection decision chooses whether it may be read, subscribed, or staged.

ResourceSelectionDecision.title: resource selection decision payload
ResourceSelectionDecision.description: As a Selection Decision, Resource selection decision is distinguished by the fact that it is a selection event that chooses, rejects, or defers a resource candidate based on relevance, authorization, freshness, data zone, and context need.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

SelectionDecision.title: Selection Decision payload
SelectionDecision.description: As an information record, Selection Decision is distinguished by the fact that it is a provenance-bearing decision over a candidate set, recording selected or rejected objects, rationale, authority/policy evidence, and decider; it follows discovery and assessment and is not a candidate.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

ToolCandidate.title: Tool Candidate payload
ToolCandidate.description: A Tool Candidate is a Candidate Record (Candidate) whose considered object is a Tool and whose matching evidence is represented by ToolMatch-assesses-ToolCandidate.

ToolCandidateSet.title: Tool Candidate Set payload
ToolCandidateSet.description: A Tool Candidate Set is a Discovery Candidate Set (DiscoveryCandidateSet) whose members are ToolCandidate records connected through ToolCandidateSet-contains-ToolCandidate.

ToolDisambiguationPrompt.title: Tool Disambiguation Prompt payload
ToolDisambiguationPrompt.description: As an information record, Tool Disambiguation Prompt is distinguished by the fact that it is a disambiguation prompt is an information artifact requesting clarification, not a candidate, match, or selection-decision subtype.

ToolEmbeddingMatch.title: Tool Embedding Match payload
ToolEmbeddingMatch.description: A Tool Embedding Match is a Tool Match Assessment (ToolMatch) whose evidence is vector similarity between a search query and a tool representation.

ToolFallback.title: Tool Fallback payload
ToolFallback.description: Tool Fallback is a specification whose defining condition is that fallback specifies an alternative rule after selection failure and can exist before a decision, so it is not a SelectionDecision subtype.

ToolMatch.title: Tool Match Assessment payload
ToolMatch.description: A Tool Match Assessment is a Match Assessment (MatchAssessment) that evaluates a ToolCandidate against a Tool using name, signature, embedding, regular-expression, or task-fit evidence.

ToolRegexMatch.title: Tool Regex Match payload
ToolRegexMatch.description: A Tool Regex Match is a Tool Match Assessment (ToolMatch) whose evidence is a regular-expression match over a query and tool metadata.

ToolSearch.title: Tool Search payload
ToolSearch.description: A Tool Search is a Discovery activity (DiscoveryActivity) that consumes a ToolSearchQuery and produces a ToolCandidateSet by retrieving ToolDefinition records.

ToolSearchQuery.title: Tool Search Query payload
ToolSearchQuery.description: Tool Search Query is an information record whose defining condition is that it is a query is an information expression consumed by search, not a subtype of the search activity or a candidate.

ToolSelectionDecision.title: Tool Selection Decision payload
ToolSelectionDecision.description: A ToolSelectionDecision is a SelectionDecision that considers ToolMatch evidence and either selects a Tool or rejects a ToolCandidate.

selection_id.title: selection-decision ID
selection_id.description: Stable identifier for one candidate assessment and selection decision.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the selection decision took effect.

reason.title: selection reason
reason.description: Explains the score, permission, and policy basis for selecting and rejecting candidates.

AgentAsToolInvocation.title: Agent-as-tool invocation payload
AgentAsToolInvocation.description: Agent-as-tool invocation is a programmatic tool call whose invoked capability is carried by an agent; the caller retains conversation control and answer responsibility, so it does not automatically constitute a delegation process.

arguments.title: arguments
arguments.description: Structured argument values validated against the referenced ToolDefinition.

call_id.title: tool-call ID
call_id.description: Stable identifier for one logical tool call shared by its retry attempts.

requested_at.title: requested at
requested_at.description: UTC instant at which the call was requested.

idempotency_key.title: idempotency key
idempotency_key.description: Caller-provided key preventing duplicate effects when supported by the tool.

caller_id.title: caller ID
caller_id.description: Identifies the actor requesting the tool call.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Pins the tool operation to execute for this call.

Command.title: Command payload
Command.description: Command is an Invocation; its differentiating condition is that it describes a tool- or runtime-level operation request that may be invoked with arguments, permissions, working directory, and observable results.

execution_area.title: Command Execution Area
execution_area.description: The execution area is a command working-directory/surface scope without independent identity and becomes a Command field.

arguments.title: Command Argument
arguments.description: An argument is a bound value of Command and is represented more accurately as a repeatable structure field.

ConditionalToolEnabled.title: conditional tool enabled payload
ConditionalToolEnabled.description: Conditional tool enabled belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is a condition specification governing when a tool is available, not a subtype of Tool, Call, or Attempt.

ExecutionRequest.title: Execution Request payload
ExecutionRequest.description: An ExecutionRequest is an InvocationSpecification that asks a runtime to run a command, call a service, access a resource, or execute a tool operation.

ExecutionResult.title: Execution Result payload
ExecutionResult.description: An ExecutionResult is an InvocationResult for an ExecutionRequest that carries observable output, error stream, exit status, or artifact references without asserting task success.

Invocation.title: Invocation payload
Invocation.description: Invocation is an activity whose defining condition is that it represents an initiated call activity with caller, target operation, and bound arguments; it is constrained by a plan and can have one or more Attempts, but is not an Attempt, Result, or Outcome.

InvocationAttempt.title: Invocation Attempt payload
InvocationAttempt.description: Invocation Attempt belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is one execution attempt for an Invocation with attempt identity, start/end time, status, timeout, and retry lineage; every retry creates a new Attempt.

InvocationDiagnostic.title: Invocation Diagnostic payload
InvocationDiagnostic.description: Invocation Diagnostic is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is call-scoped information explaining error, warning, timeout, or partial execution; a non-fatal diagnostic can coexist with InvocationResult, and the occurrence can map to a Feedback FailureEvent.

InvocationEffect.title: Invocation Effect payload
InvocationEffect.description: Invocation Effect is an event; its differentiating condition is that it records an external, persistent, or compensable change produced or attempted by an invocation attempt; it can coexist with a return result and connects by governance relations to approval, audit, and recovery.

InvocationExecutionRequest.title: Invocation execution request payload
InvocationExecutionRequest.description: Invocation execution request is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is an addressable instance of an invocation specification that binds arguments, requester, idempotency key, and applicable runtime context.

request_id.title: request ID
request_id.description: Uniquely identifies one invocation execution request.

InvocationResult.title: Invocation Result payload
InvocationResult.description: Invocation Result belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is the direct return record of an Attempt, storing content, status, metadata, and provenance; diagnostics may accompany it, side effects may be caused by the same Attempt, and business Outcome is interpreted at a higher level.

InvocationSpecification.title: Invocation Specification payload
InvocationSpecification.description: Invocation Specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is pre-call static or planning information specifying operation, arguments, approval, timeout, idempotency, and result handling; it does not assert that a call occurred.

PreExecutionSafetyCheck.title: pre-execution safety check payload
PreExecutionSafetyCheck.description: Pre-execution safety check belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that the safety check is a pre-execution governance activity related to a plan, not a subtype of the plan or call.

ProgrammaticToolCall.title: Programmatic Tool Call payload
ProgrammaticToolCall.description: A ProgrammaticToolCall is a ToolCall initiated by generated code, runtime orchestration, or explicit API logic rather than direct natural-language selection.

arguments.title: arguments
arguments.description: Structured argument values validated against the referenced ToolDefinition.

call_id.title: tool-call ID
call_id.description: Stable identifier for one logical tool call shared by its retry attempts.

requested_at.title: requested at
requested_at.description: UTC instant at which the call was requested.

idempotency_key.title: idempotency key
idempotency_key.description: Caller-provided key preventing duplicate effects when supported by the tool.

caller_id.title: caller ID
caller_id.description: Identifies the actor requesting the tool call.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Pins the tool operation to execute for this call.

PromptGetRequest.title: prompt get request payload
PromptGetRequest.description: Prompt get request is an Invocation; its differentiating condition is that it is an event request to retrieve a prompt definition or template from a registry or protocol server before argument binding and context staging.

PromptInstantiation.title: prompt instantiation payload
PromptInstantiation.description: Prompt instantiation is the activity of binding arguments to a definition, not a subtype of template, call, or result.

ResourceReadRequest.title: resource read request payload
ResourceReadRequest.description: Resource read request belongs to the Invocation category and differs from broader members in that it is an event request to read a named resource from a server or connector, including resource identity, access scope, authorization context, and expected representation.

ResourceReadResult.title: resource read result payload
ResourceReadResult.description: Resource read result is an Invocation Result; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable resource content and metadata returned by a resource read request, including representation, provenance, cache hints, warning state, and context-ingress eligibility.

ResourceSubscription.title: resource subscription payload
ResourceSubscription.description: Resource subscription belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is an identified ongoing specification of selectors, delivery, and cancellation, not a single invocation.

ShellCommand.title: Shell Command payload
ShellCommand.description: Shell Command is a Command; its differentiating condition is that it describes a shell-level command invocation with command text, arguments, environment, working directory, and sandbox or permission context.

execution_area.title: Command Execution Area
execution_area.description: The execution area is a command working-directory/surface scope without independent identity and becomes a Command field.

arguments.title: Command Argument
arguments.description: An argument is a bound value of Command and is represented more accurately as a repeatable structure field.

ToolApprovalGate.title: tool approval gate payload
ToolApprovalGate.description: As a specification, Tool approval gate is distinguished by the fact that it is a pre-execution control specification referencing safety authorization decisions, not a Call or Attempt subtype.

ToolCall.title: Tool Call payload
ToolCall.description: A ToolCall is an Invocation that identifies a caller and operation, supplies arguments, and requests a selected Tool; call_id, requested_at, operation_id, and idempotency_key make the request traceable and replay-safe.

arguments.title: arguments
arguments.description: Structured argument values validated against the referenced ToolDefinition.

call_id.title: tool-call ID
call_id.description: Stable identifier for one logical tool call shared by its retry attempts.

requested_at.title: requested at
requested_at.description: UTC instant at which the call was requested.

idempotency_key.title: idempotency key
idempotency_key.description: Caller-provided key preventing duplicate effects when supported by the tool.

caller_id.title: caller ID
caller_id.description: Identifies the actor requesting the tool call.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Pins the tool operation to execute for this call.

ToolCallAttempt.title: Tool Call Attempt payload
ToolCallAttempt.description: A ToolCallAttempt is an InvocationAttempt that records one execution attempt of a ToolCall, identified by attempt_id and attempt_number, with status, timing, authorization, sandbox, and result context.

status.title: attempt status
status.description: A tool-call attempt uses the generic run-attempt controlled lifecycle states and requires every attempt record to state one explicitly.

duration_ms.title: Duration milliseconds
duration_ms.description: captures duration in milliseconds as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

retry_count.title: Retry count
retry_count.description: captures retry count as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

legacy_status_text.title: Legacy status text
legacy_status_text.description: captures status as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

attempt_number.title: attempt number
attempt_number.description: One-based attempt number within the same logical call.

retry_of.title: retry of
retry_of.description: If this attempt is a retry, references its immediate predecessor; empty for the initial attempt.

attempt_id.title: attempt ID
attempt_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual execution attempt; every retry gets a new identifier.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which this execution attempt started.

timeout_ms.title: timeout milliseconds
timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds this attempt may run before timing out.

sandbox_id.title: sandbox ID
sandbox_id.description: Identifies the sandbox isolating process, filesystem, and network for this attempt.

authorization_id.title: authorization ID
authorization_id.description: Identifies the authorization grant or approval governing this attempt.

ToolCallPlan.title: tool call plan payload
ToolCallPlan.description: Tool call plan belongs to the Invocation Specification category and differs from broader members in that it is a planned invocation artifact that proposes a candidate tool action, constructed arguments, approval requirements, expected result handling, retry policy, and pre-execution safety context.

ToolCallRetry.title: Tool Call Retry payload
ToolCallRetry.description: A Tool Call Retry is a Tool Call Attempt (ToolCallAttempt) initiated to retry a prior ToolCallAttempt after a retryable failure or policy decision and linked by ToolCallRetry-retries-ToolCallAttempt.

status.title: attempt status
status.description: A tool-call attempt uses the generic run-attempt controlled lifecycle states and requires every attempt record to state one explicitly.

duration_ms.title: Duration milliseconds
duration_ms.description: captures duration in milliseconds as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

retry_count.title: Retry count
retry_count.description: captures retry count as a integer scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

legacy_status_text.title: Legacy status text
legacy_status_text.description: captures status as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

attempt_number.title: attempt number
attempt_number.description: One-based attempt number within the same logical call.

retry_of.title: retry of
retry_of.description: If this attempt is a retry, references its immediate predecessor; empty for the initial attempt.

attempt_id.title: attempt ID
attempt_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual execution attempt; every retry gets a new identifier.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which this execution attempt started.

timeout_ms.title: timeout milliseconds
timeout_ms.description: Maximum milliseconds this attempt may run before timing out.

sandbox_id.title: sandbox ID
sandbox_id.description: Identifies the sandbox isolating process, filesystem, and network for this attempt.

authorization_id.title: authorization ID
authorization_id.description: Identifies the authorization grant or approval governing this attempt.

ToolDescriptionTrust.title: tool description trust payload
ToolDescriptionTrust.description: As a quality characteristic, Tool description trust is distinguished by the fact that description trust is a governed quality attached to a tool description or source, not a definition or invocation stage.

ToolError.title: tool error payload
ToolError.description: As an Invocation Diagnostic, Tool error is distinguished by the fact that it is a diagnostic resource describing an error, exception, timeout, validation failure, or execution failure associated with a tool call attempt.

ToolResult.title: Tool Result payload
ToolResult.description: A ToolResult is an InvocationResult produced by a ToolCallAttempt, identified by result_id, status, received_at, and payload_digest, and carrying output, error, warning, or metadata without asserting task success.

result_id.title: result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for the tool result returned by one call attempt.

status.title: result status
status.description: Distinguishes success, partial success, or protocol-level failure results.

received_at.title: received at
received_at.description: UTC instant at which the runtime received the result.

payload_digest.title: payload digest
payload_digest.description: Immutable content digest of the raw result payload for integrity and deduplication.

ToolResultObservation.title: Tool Result Observation payload
ToolResultObservation.description: Tool Result Observation belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that the observation records how a result is interpreted, traced, or staged into context and has provenance distinct from the raw result.

ToolSideEffect.title: tool side effect payload
ToolSideEffect.description: As an Invocation Effect, Tool side effect is distinguished by the fact that it is a tool-local record of an external state change or persistent effect produced or attempted by a tool execution before it is connected to commit approval, rollback, audit, and canonical safety SideEffect evidence.

ToolWarning.title: tool warning payload
ToolWarning.description: As an Invocation Diagnostic, Tool warning is distinguished by the fact that it is a non-fatal warning or diagnostic resource attached to a tool result, argument validation, degraded execution, fallback, unsafe content signal, or partial response.

UnsafeArgumentPattern.title: unsafe argument pattern payload
UnsafeArgumentPattern.description: Unsafe argument pattern is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a versionable detection/governance specification related to arguments, not an Argument or Call subtype.

AdditionalInput.title: additional input payload
AdditionalInput.description: Additional input belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is data requested by elicitation, not a subtype of interaction, participant, or capability.

MCPAuthorization.title: MCP authorization payload
MCPAuthorization.description: MCP authorization belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is consent, grant, or scope evidence carried by a session, not a participant or transport.

MCPCapability.title: MCP Capability payload
MCPCapability.description: MCP Capability is a capability whose defining condition is that it represents a protocol function declared through initialization or negotiation, such as tools, resources, prompts, sampling, roots, or elicitation support; capability declarations are distinct from lists containing definitions.

MCPClient.title: MCP client payload
MCPClient.description: MCP client is a MCP Participant; its differentiating condition is that it is a protocol participant object that opens MCP sessions, discovers server capabilities, requests prompts or resources, invokes tools, and carries authorization context for an agent runtime.

MCPDefinitionList.title: MCP Definition List payload
MCPDefinitionList.description: MCPDefinitionList is a collection with list version, pagination, or change-notification semantics whose members are tool, resource, template, prompt, or root entries; a capability enables publication, but the list is not itself a capability.

MCPElicitation.title: MCP Elicitation payload
MCPElicitation.description: MCP Elicitation is an MCP request (MCPRequest) in which an MCP server asks a client or user for AdditionalInput during an active MCPInteraction, subject to client consent and policy.

MCPInteraction.title: MCP Interaction payload
MCPInteraction.description: MCPInteraction records protocol request/response correlation, direction, participants, time, and result state within a session; sampling and elicitation specialize by purpose, while input content and authorization evidence connect by relations.

MCPMessage.title: MCP message payload
MCPMessage.description: MCP message belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is an identified protocol record transported within one negotiated version and session, fixing message ID, sender, receiver, time, and payload type; transport specification remains separate from the message record.

MCPNotification.title: MCP notification payload
MCPNotification.description: MCP notification is a MCP message; its differentiating condition is that it is a one-way message sent within an active session or subscription scope that expects no paired response.

MCPParticipant.title: MCP Participant payload
MCPParticipant.description: MCPParticipant is a role joining an MCP session with endpoint identity and sending or receiving protocol messages; Client and Server specialize it by responsibility and message direction.

MCPPromptList.title: MCP prompt list payload
MCPPromptList.description: MCP prompt list is a MCP Definition List whose defining condition is that it is a server-exposed list of prompt definitions and prompt templates available for retrieval, argument binding, workflow staging, or guided interaction.

MCPRequest.title: MCP request payload
MCPRequest.description: MCP request belongs to the MCP message category and differs from broader members in that it asks the peer to perform one declared operation and return a terminal response under the same session correlation ID.

MCPResourceList.title: MCP resource list payload
MCPResourceList.description: As a MCP Definition List, MCP resource list is distinguished by the fact that it is a server-exposed list of resource definitions and resource templates available for read, subscribe, discovery, or context/data access operations.

MCPResourceTemplateList.title: MCP resource template list payload
MCPResourceTemplateList.description: As a MCP Definition List, MCP resource template list is distinguished by the fact that it is a server-exposed list of resource templates whose parameters can be bound to discover or read families of MCP resources.

MCPResponse.title: MCP response payload
MCPResponse.description: MCP response is a MCP message; its differentiating condition is that it is a message that terminates one request by correlation ID and carries either a result or protocol error.

MCPRootList.title: MCP root list payload
MCPRootList.description: As a MCP Definition List, MCP root list is distinguished by the fact that it is a client-provided list of roots or workspace scopes that bound which files, directories, repositories, or resource regions an MCP server may access.

MCPSamplingRequest.title: MCP sampling request payload
MCPSamplingRequest.description: As a MCP request, MCP sampling request is distinguished by the fact that it is a protocol request in which an MCP server asks the client or host to perform model sampling under the client's policy, context, and authorization controls.

MCPServer.title: MCP server payload
MCPServer.description: MCP server is a MCP Participant; its differentiating condition is that it is a protocol participant object that exposes tools, resources, prompts, templates, and capability metadata to MCP clients over a session and transport.

MCPServerCapability.title: MCP server capability payload
MCPServerCapability.description: MCP server capability belongs to the MCP Capability category and differs from broader members in that it is a capability metadata advertised by an MCP server, including supported tool, resource, prompt, sampling, roots, elicitation, authorization, and transport features.

MCPSession.title: MCP Session payload
MCPSession.description: As an entity, MCP Session is distinguished by the fact that it records the MCP client/server connection context in which capabilities, requests, responses, authorization, and transport metadata are exchanged.

mcp_session_id.title: MCP session ID
mcp_session_id.description: Stable identifier for one MCP session between a client and server.

protocol_version.title: protocol version
protocol_version.description: MCP protocol version negotiated during initialization and used by this session.

state.title: session state
state.description: State in the initialized, active, closed, or failed MCP lifecycle.

MCPSubscription.title: MCP subscription payload
MCPSubscription.description: MCP subscription belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is an immutable record granting notification scope for specified topics, resources, and validity within one session; later cancellation or disconnect terminates its validity by a new event.

MCPToolList.title: MCP tool list payload
MCPToolList.description: MCP tool list is a MCP Definition List whose defining condition is that it is a server-exposed list of tool definitions and callable capability descriptors available for discovery, filtering, approval, and invocation by an MCP client.

MCPTransport.title: MCP transport payload
MCPTransport.description: MCP transport belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is a protocol channel and delivery surface used by MCP client/server sessions to exchange JSON-RPC messages, capability metadata, tool calls, resource reads, prompt requests, and elicitation traffic.

transport_kind.title: Transport kind
transport_kind.description: Identifies the negotiated transport mechanism used by an MCP session.

APIOperation.title: API operation payload
APIOperation.description: An APIOperation is a ToolCapability representing an external API action with a method, endpoint, arguments schema, result schema, authentication requirements, side-effect expectations, and observable invocation result.

CapabilityDescriptor.title: capability descriptor payload
CapabilityDescriptor.description: A CapabilityDescriptor is a ToolMetadata record that provides a machine-readable account of operation identity, schema surface, trust metadata, permission requirements, discovery labels, and protocol exposure for a tool, resource, prompt, or API operation.

CapabilitySurface.title: capability surface payload
CapabilitySurface.description: Capability surface is a collection; its differentiating condition is that it contains discoverable CapabilityDescriptor records under environment, protocol-version, and authorization scope; RegistryPublicationActivity performs publication, while the collection itself has no publishing agency.

ComputerTool.title: computer tool payload
ComputerTool.description: Computer tool belongs to the Tool category and differs from broader members in that it is an object type for a computer, browser, desktop UI, or screen-control tool that acts on an interactive environment and produces observable UI or environment state.

DeregistrationActivity.title: Deregistration activity payload
DeregistrationActivity.description: Deregistration activity belongs to the Registry activity category and differs from broader members in that it terminates future validity without deleting the historical entry.

FunctionTool.title: function tool payload
FunctionTool.description: A FunctionTool is a Tool whose invocation is described by a callable name, arguments schema, return schema, and host-side implementation.

HostedMCPTool.title: hosted MCP tool payload
HostedMCPTool.description: Hosted MCP tool belongs to the hosted tool category and differs from broader members in that it is an object type for an MCP-hosted tool exposed by a server to a client along with protocol metadata, authorization context, schema surface, and invocation constraints.

HostedTool.title: hosted tool payload
HostedTool.description: A HostedTool is a Tool backed by an external service or provider-managed runtime whose execution surface is invoked through a registry or protocol connector.

LocalRuntimeTool.title: local runtime tool payload
LocalRuntimeTool.description: A LocalRuntimeTool is a Tool that executes through a host-controlled process, shell, computer-control, code, browser, or container surface.

PromptDefinition.title: prompt definition payload
PromptDefinition.description: Prompt definition is a Tool Registry Specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a resource that describes a reusable prompt template and workflow pattern, expected arguments, output role, and applicability conditions exposed by a server or registry.

RegistrationActivity.title: Registration activity payload
RegistrationActivity.description: As a Registry activity, Registration activity is distinguished by the fact that it validates provider and contract and then produces a new immutable registry entry.

RegistryActivity.title: Registry activity payload
RegistryActivity.description: Registry activity is an activity whose defining condition is that it is an auditable activity that creates or terminates a registry entry's validity under registry governance rules.

RegistryEntry.title: Registry entry payload
RegistryEntry.description: Registry entry is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is an immutable registration record publishing one capability specification for an environment and validity interval, fixing provider, endpoint, contract version, and status evidence; it is not the capability definition itself.

registry_entry_id.title: registry entry
registry_entry_id.description: This field uniquely identifies the registry entry record.

environment_scope.title: environment scope
environment_scope.description: Deployment environment in which the entry can resolve.

endpoint_reference.title: endpoint reference
endpoint_reference.description: Reference to the resolvable endpoint providing the capability implementation.

RegistryPublicationActivity.title: Registry publication activity payload
RegistryPublicationActivity.description: As a Registry activity, Registry publication activity is distinguished by the fact that it validates descriptor, environment scope, protocol version, and authorization conditions and publishes the validated capability descriptor to a capability surface.

ResourceDefinition.title: resource definition payload
ResourceDefinition.description: Resource definition belongs to the Tool Registry Specification category and differs from broader members in that it describes how a protocol or server exposes context/data for list, read, subscribe, and resource-template operations.

ResourceTemplate.title: resource template payload
ResourceTemplate.description: A ResourceTemplate is a ResourceDefinition that binds variables to resource URIs or selectors so a parameterized family of related resources can be discovered and read safely.

ShellTool.title: shell tool payload
ShellTool.description: Shell tool is a local runtime tool whose defining condition is that it is an object type for a shell or command runtime tool that executes command lines, scripts, or process operations under sandbox and policy constraints.

Tool.title: Tool payload
Tool.description: A Tool is an entity that exposes an APIOperation as a callable capability, accepts arguments under its tool definition and permission constraints, and yields an observable result when invoked.

ToolCapability.title: Tool Capability payload
ToolCapability.description: As a capability, Tool Capability is distinguished by the fact that it is an advertised operation or capability surface of a tool, including its execution boundary, permission requirements, schema surface, and observable result type.

ToolDefinition.title: tool definition payload
ToolDefinition.description: Tool definition is a Tool Registry Specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a schema-like resource describing a tool's name, purpose, callable operation, argument schema, result schema, permission declaration, constraints, version, and deprecation state.

argument_schema.title: argument structure
argument_schema.description: Argument structure embedded as node information; it does not create a Schema graph node.

description.title: Tool Description
description.description: The legacy item is only definition text without separate identity or provenance, so it becomes ToolDefinition.description.

name.title: tool name
name.description: Stable name by which callers refer to the tool.

result_schema.title: result structure
result_schema.description: Result structure embedded as node information; it does not create a Schema graph node.

version.title: definition version
version.description: Identifies one immutable version of the argument, result, and permission contract.

definition_id.title: definition ID
definition_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical tool definition across versions.

ToolDeprecationNotice.title: Tool Deprecation Notice payload
ToolDeprecationNotice.description: A ToolDeprecationNotice is ToolMetadata that identifies a deprecated ToolDefinition and records lifecycle or replacement guidance rather than describing the callable capability itself.

ToolMetadata.title: Tool Metadata Record payload
ToolMetadata.description: Tool Metadata Record is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is a separately referencable, versioned, or governed descriptive record such as a capability descriptor or deprecation notice; ordinary names, version strings, and description text remain ToolDefinition fields.

ToolPermissionSpec.title: tool permission spec payload
ToolPermissionSpec.description: Tool permission spec is a Tool Registry Specification whose defining condition is that it is a tool-side permission declaration that states required scopes, approval behavior, protected resources, safety constraints, and policy hooks without replacing a policy decision.

ToolRegistry.title: tool registry payload
ToolRegistry.description: Tool registry is a collection; its differentiating condition is that it is a catalog resource that stores available tool definitions, capability descriptors, schemas, permissions, versions, discovery metadata, and protocol exposure records.

ToolSpecification.title: Tool Registry Specification payload
ToolSpecification.description: Tool Registry Specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that ToolSpecification is a static registry specification with stable identity, version, and constraints; it can specify how a tool, prompt, or resource is discovered and used, but is not a runtime call, attempt, or result.

CommitApproval.title: commit approval payload
CommitApproval.description: Commit approval is an allow decision whose defining condition is that it records an approval outcome that authorizes a bounded side effect, including approver or policy authority, protected resource, scope, conditions, expiry, and trace evidence.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

CommitDenial.title: commit denial payload
CommitDenial.description: Commit denial is a deny decision whose defining condition is that it records a denial outcome that blocks a requested side effect, including policy basis, denial reason, affected resource, actor, and audit trace.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

CommitGate.title: side-effect commit gate payload
CommitGate.description: Side-effect commit gate is a gate whose defining condition is that it represents a side-effect gate that blocks or delays writes, external actions, network effects, commits, or disclosures until policy checks, approvals, or rollback controls are satisfied.

CommitRequest.title: commit request payload
CommitRequest.description: Commit request is an information record whose defining condition is that it records a request to perform a side effect such as file write, repository commit, network mutation, external API action, memory write, or disclosure release.

EffectReceipt.title: External effect receipt payload
EffectReceipt.description: External effect receipt belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is immutable audit information by which an external system confirms that a side effect was actually committed, fixing receipt identity, idempotency key, effect digest, commit time, and external confirmation identifier; a preview result cannot serve as a receipt.

receipt_id.title: receipt ID
receipt_id.description: Stable unique identifier of the external-effect receipt.

idempotency_key.title: idempotency key
idempotency_key.description: Key preventing duplicate commit of the same external effect within an authority scope.

effect_digest.title: effect digest
effect_digest.description: Digest of the normalized representation of the confirmed external effect.

SideEffect.title: side effect payload
SideEffect.description: Side effect is an event whose defining condition is that it records an externally visible or persistent effect produced by a tool call, sandbox command, network call, memory write, file write, commit, or protocol operation.

AuditDisclosure.title: audit disclosure payload
AuditDisclosure.description: As an information record, Audit disclosure is distinguished by the fact that it records the auditable disclosure decision for released, redacted, or suppressed content, including filter, policy, recipient boundary, sensitive span, and trace evidence.

DisclosureControlActivity.title: disclosure-control activity payload
DisclosureControlActivity.description: Disclosure-control activity is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it is performed by an identified actor for a stated recipient and trust boundary, applying disclosure rules to output, messages, artifacts, traces, or memory content. It records input content, sensitive spans, rule version, selection outcome, generated content, and audit evidence. Rules and result information are not activity subtypes.

DisclosureFilter.title: disclosure filter payload
DisclosureFilter.description: Disclosure filter is a disclosure-control activity; its differentiating condition is that it applies disclosure policy to output windows, context packages, artifacts, traces, or messages, deciding whether content is released, redacted, suppressed, or escalated.

DisclosureRule.title: disclosure rule payload
DisclosureRule.description: Disclosure rule belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it defines a safety or policy rule that determines when content may be revealed, redacted, delayed, summarized, or blocked for a recipient.

ProgressiveDisclosure.title: progressive disclosure payload
ProgressiveDisclosure.description: Progressive disclosure is a disclosure-control activity whose defining condition is that it records a controlled release pattern in which content is revealed in stages under policy, trust-boundary, and context-window constraints.

Redaction.title: redaction activity payload
Redaction.description: Redaction activity belongs to the disclosure-control activity category and differs from broader members in that it records the operation that masks, removes, replaces, summarizes, or withholds sensitive content before it is shown, exported, stored, or sent across a boundary.

redaction_id.title: redaction ID
redaction_id.description: Stable identifier for one auditable information-redaction action.

applied_at.title: applied at
applied_at.description: UTC instant at which the redaction rule was applied.

replacement_marker.title: replacement marker
replacement_marker.description: Display marker replacing sensitive content without disclosing it.

RedactionRule.title: redaction rule payload
RedactionRule.description: Redaction rule belongs to the disclosure rule category and differs from broader members in that it defines a policy rule for detecting, masking, suppressing, summarizing, or replacing sensitive spans before content crosses an output or trust boundary.

SensitiveSpan.title: sensitive span payload
SensitiveSpan.description: As an information record, Sensitive span is distinguished by the fact that it identifies a span of output, source, message, trace, artifact, or memory content that requires redaction, suppression, masking, delayed disclosure, or restricted routing.

SuppressedOutput.title: suppressed output payload
SuppressedOutput.description: Suppressed output is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it records output withheld from a viewer or downstream context because of policy, privacy, safety, boundary, or budget constraints.

DefenseFinding.title: defense finding payload
DefenseFinding.description: Defense finding belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records a safety finding emitted by injection defense or taint analysis before it is converted into feedback metrics, review findings, blocking errors, or policy decisions.

risk_level.title: Risk level
risk_level.description: Represents the reviewed severity of a defense finding.

finding_id.title: defense-finding ID
finding_id.description: Stable identifier for one security finding formed by scanning or taint analysis.

confidence.title: confidence
confidence.description: Zero-to-one confidence assigned by the detection activity to the finding.

risk_score.title: risk score
risk_score.description: Zero-to-one risk score combining impact and likelihood.

observed_at.title: observed at
observed_at.description: UTC instant at which the detection activity established the finding.

DetectionActivity.title: detection activity payload
DetectionActivity.description: As an activity, Detection activity is distinguished by the fact that it is performed by an identified detector at a stated time over content, messages, tool output, schemas, memory, or propagation paths. It has an input subject, method, configuration version, and execution context and produces separate scan results or findings; those results are not activity subtypes.

IndirectInjectionSignal.title: indirect injection signal payload
IndirectInjectionSignal.description: Indirect injection signal is a threat signal; its differentiating condition is that it records evidence that untrusted external content can influence agent behavior after retrieval, summarization, memory persistence, tool observation, or protocol translation.

InjectionScanResult.title: injection scan result payload
InjectionScanResult.description: Injection scan result is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it records the result of scanning content for injection or taint risk, including matched signal, affected span, source, confidence, policy decision, and downstream sink if known.

InjectionSignature.title: injection signature specification payload
InjectionSignature.description: An Injection signature specification is a specification that defines a detection pattern for indicators of prompt injection, tool-stream manipulation, or malicious instruction propagation and serves as the target of scans_for_signature.

InstructionConflict.title: instruction conflict payload
InstructionConflict.description: As an information record, Instruction conflict is distinguished by the fact that it records a safety-relevant conflict between trusted instructions and untrusted or lower-authority instruction candidates, including affected scope and resolution.

MaliciousToolOutput.title: malicious tool output payload
MaliciousToolOutput.description: Malicious tool output belongs to the poisoned content category and differs from broader members in that it records tool, resource, sandbox, or remote-agent output that carries untrusted instructions, exfiltration cues, policy-bypass attempts, deceptive tool guidance, or prompt-injection content.

MemoryPoisoningSignal.title: memory poisoning signal payload
MemoryPoisoningSignal.description: Memory poisoning signal belongs to the threat signal category and differs from broader members in that it records evidence that persistent memory, retrieved chunks, indexes, summaries, or long-term context may contain instructions or data intended to corrupt later agent behavior.

MitigationAction.title: mitigation action payload
MitigationAction.description: Mitigation action is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it is a controlled disposition triggered by a policy decision or authorization against an identified subject and risk. It has actor, time, scope, before/after state, validation, and failure or rollback outcome. Sanitization and quarantine specialize it; trusted-instruction override remains a governance-decision record rather than being forced into the activity hierarchy.

PatternScan.title: pattern scan payload
PatternScan.description: A PatternScan is a DetectionActivity that examines messages, retrieved chunks, tool results, or other untrusted content for InjectionSignature indicators or policy violations and produces an InjectionScanResult.

PersistentContextRisk.title: persistent context risk payload
PersistentContextRisk.description: Persistent context risk is a quality characteristic; its differentiating condition is that it records a risk that unsafe, stale, adversarial, or over-authoritative content will persist across sessions, retrieval, summaries, checkpoints, or memory writes.

PoisonedContent.title: poisoned content payload
PoisonedContent.description: As an information record, Poisoned content is distinguished by the fact that it is an information object with provenance, affected range, poisoning category, detection evidence, and disposition state. Its text or structured fields contain adversarial material intended to alter instruction priority, tool selection, arguments, disclosure, or persistent memory. Poisoning and detection events connect by relation and are not members of this class.

PoisonedRetrievedChunk.title: poisoned retrieved chunk payload
PoisonedRetrievedChunk.description: As a poisoned content, Poisoned retrieved chunk is distinguished by the fact that it identifies a retrieved memory or document chunk that contains unsafe instructions, misleading metadata, tainted spans, or prompt-injection content.

PoisonedToolDescription.title: poisoned tool description payload
PoisonedToolDescription.description: Poisoned tool description is a poisoned content; its differentiating condition is that it identifies a tool description, MCP tool list entry, capability advertisement, or help text that attempts to manipulate selection, arguments, permissions, or trust.

PromptInjectionSignal.title: prompt injection signal payload
PromptInjectionSignal.description: Prompt injection signal is a threat signal; its differentiating condition is that it records evidence that a message, source span, retrieved content, or tool result contains instructions attempting to override trusted instructions, manipulate tool arguments, exfiltrate data, or bypass policy.

QuarantineAction.title: quarantine action payload
QuarantineAction.description: Quarantine action is a mitigation action whose defining condition is that it records an action that isolates content, tool output, retrieved memory, or artifact from normal context and execution flow until review or policy resolution.

ResourceContentPoisoning.title: resource content poisoning payload
ResourceContentPoisoning.description: Resource content poisoning belongs to the event category and differs from broader members in that ResourceContentPoisoning denotes an event or occurrence record that introduces adversarial content into a resource, not the PoisonedContent itself; a relation should point to the affected content.

RiskFlowRole.title: risk-flow role payload
RiskFlowRole.description: Risk-flow role is a role; its differentiating condition is that it is a context-dependent role stating that a message, tool output, memory item, argument, credentialed operation, or side effect acts as risk source or protected destination within a SourceSinkFlow. The same entity may bear different roles in different flows without changing its ontological type.

RiskSink.title: risk sink payload
RiskSink.description: Risk sink is a risk-flow role whose defining condition is that it classifies the protected destination that tainted content may influence, such as tool invocation, credentialed request, filesystem write, memory update, network call, or final disclosure.

RiskSource.title: risk source payload
RiskSource.description: Risk source is a risk-flow role whose defining condition is that it classifies the origin of a safety risk, such as external message, retrieved document, tool output, remote agent artifact, memory item, network response, or user-supplied file.

SanitizationAction.title: sanitization action payload
SanitizationAction.description: Sanitization action is a mitigation action; its differentiating condition is that it records an action that removes, rewrites, masks, neutralizes, or narrows unsafe content before it can affect context, tool arguments, memory, or disclosure.

SourceSinkFlow.title: source sink flow payload
SourceSinkFlow.description: Source sink flow is an information record whose defining condition is that it records a source-to-sink risk path from untrusted content to a protected sink such as tool arguments, network calls, memory writes, side effects, or disclosed output.

TaintedSource.title: tainted source payload
TaintedSource.description: Tainted source is a taint-bearing entity whose defining condition is that it identifies an untrusted or policy-sensitive source whose content must be tracked before it enters context, memory, a tool argument, a network request, or disclosed output.

TaintedSpan.title: tainted span payload
TaintedSpan.description: Tainted span is a taint-bearing entity; its differentiating condition is that it identifies the exact span of message, source, retrieved chunk, tool result, or output segment that carries tainted content for downstream source-sink analysis.

TaintEntity.title: taint-bearing entity payload
TaintEntity.description: Taint-bearing entity belongs to the entity category and differs from broader members in that it is a TaintEntity is a referenceable object assigned a taint identifier by detection or policy. It has provenance, taint category, confidence, exact range, lifecycle, and clearance conditions. Whole sources and precise spans may specialize it, while propagation actions and source/sink roles do not.

TaintPropagation.title: taint propagation payload
TaintPropagation.description: As an event, Taint propagation is distinguished by the fact that it reifies the propagation of taint from one source span, context package, memory item, tool result, or artifact to another observable object.

ThreatSignal.title: threat signal payload
ThreatSignal.description: Threat signal is an information record whose defining condition is that it is evidence information with detector source, inspected subject, evidence location, threat category, confidence, and time. It indicates that prompts, external content, tool streams, or memory may carry injection or poisoning risk. It supports findings and decisions but is not a confirmed threat, risk state, or mitigation action.

ToolSchemaPoisoning.title: tool schema poisoning payload
ToolSchemaPoisoning.description: Tool schema poisoning belongs to the event category and differs from broader members in that ToolSchemaPoisoning denotes an event record of adversarial changes to schema, defaults, or descriptions; affected poisoned content is linked by relation rather than treating the process as content.

ToolStreamInjectionSignal.title: tool stream injection signal payload
ToolStreamInjectionSignal.description: Tool stream injection signal is a threat signal; its differentiating condition is that it records evidence that streamed tool output, tool result messages, logs, or remote artifacts attempt to inject instructions into later context, tool arguments, routing, or disclosure decisions.

TrustedInstructionOverride.title: trusted instruction override payload
TrustedInstructionOverride.description: Trusted instruction override belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records that a higher-authority instruction, policy, or approval overrides an untrusted or lower-authority instruction candidate for a bounded scope.

UntrustedInstructionCandidate.title: untrusted instruction candidate payload
UntrustedInstructionCandidate.description: Untrusted instruction candidate belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records an instruction-like text or structured directive from an untrusted source before it is accepted, ignored, quarantined, overridden, or escalated.

DeniedNetworkCall.title: Denied network-call event payload
DeniedNetworkCall.description: Denied network-call event is an event; its differentiating condition is that it records that policy prevented a request before a call to an accessible endpoint was established, retaining the policy, reason, and request evidence.

DomainSocket.title: domain socket payload
DomainSocket.description: A DomainSocket is a Socket that records use of a local inter-process communication endpoint whose boundary, permission, credential, and sandbox implications must be explicit.

HTTPProxy.title: HTTP proxy payload
