HTTPProxy.description: HTTP proxy is a proxy endpoint whose defining condition is that it represents an HTTP or HTTPS proxy route used to mediate requests and responses under egress policy, credential scope, and disclosure controls.

InboundResponse.title: inbound response payload
InboundResponse.description: An InboundResponse is an information record that records a response returned across a boundary, including its origin, response data zone, status, tool or protocol context, injection-scan result, and disclosure handling.

NetworkCall.title: network call payload
NetworkCall.description: Network call is a network interaction whose defining condition is that it records an attempted or completed network operation with destination, protocol, method, credential scope, proxy route, data zone, policy decision, and trace evidence.

network_call_id.title: network-call ID
network_call_id.description: Stable identifier for one actual network-interaction attempt.

method.title: method
method.description: Request method or operation name used by the network protocol.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which the network call started.

status.title: call status
status.description: Whether the call was allowed, denied, succeeded, failed, or timed out.

NetworkEndpoint.title: network communication endpoint payload
NetworkEndpoint.description: Network communication endpoint is an entity; its differentiating condition is that it is a NetworkEndpoint is a communication entity with addressable identity, protocol, binding location, trust-boundary ownership, and applicable network policy. It may accept communication directly or forward traffic for a target. Proxies, sockets, and their protocol specializations qualify; requests and responses do not.

NetworkInteraction.title: network interaction payload
NetworkInteraction.description: Network interaction is an event whose defining condition is that it is an observable communication event with initiator, target endpoint, protocol, time, trust boundary, credential scope, policy decision, and outcome. NetworkCall and denied calls may specialize it, while OutboundRequest and InboundResponse are information carried by the interaction and connect through relations.

NetworkPolicy.title: network policy payload
NetworkPolicy.description: A network policy is a policy specification that defines allow, deny, or escalation decisions over endpoint, protocol, direction, identity, and environmental conditions; an isolation specification may reference it but does not own its identity.

NetworkResource.title: network resource payload
NetworkResource.description: Network resource is an entity whose defining condition is that it identifies a network-originated resource or endpoint whose content, credentials, response data zone, and trust boundary affect agent safety decisions.

OutboundRequest.title: outbound request payload
OutboundRequest.description: An OutboundRequest is an information record that stores a boundary-crossing request from a tool, sandbox, protocol adapter, or remote-agent interaction, including target endpoint, method, credential scope, data zone, and policy decision.

Proxy.title: proxy endpoint payload
Proxy.description: Proxy endpoint is a network communication endpoint whose defining condition is that it represents a controlled proxy surface that mediates outbound or inbound traffic according to network policy, credential scope, logging, and data-zone constraints.

ProxyRoute.title: proxy route record payload
ProxyRoute.description: Proxy route record belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records the controlled proxy path used for network traffic, including proxy kind, target endpoint, allowed protocol, credential boundary, egress policy, and trace evidence.

Socket.title: socket endpoint payload
Socket.description: Socket endpoint belongs to the network communication endpoint category and differs from broader members in that it records a communication endpoint used by sandboxed processes, proxies, tools, or network calls and links it to protocol, route, policy, and trace evidence.

SOCKSProxy.title: SOCKS proxy payload
SOCKSProxy.description: A SOCKSProxy is a proxy endpoint that represents a SOCKS route used to mediate network egress while preserving policy, target, credential, and audit metadata.

AllowDecision.title: allow decision payload
AllowDecision.description: Allow decision belongs to the policy decision category and differs from broader members in that it records a policy decision that permits a bounded operation to proceed under stated scope, conditions, trace evidence, and expiry.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

AuthorizationArtifact.title: authorization artifact payload
AuthorizationArtifact.description: Authorization artifact is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is an auditable and revocable information artifact issued by an identifiable authority. It records the grantee, permitted actions, protected resources, context, permission scope, issue and expiry times, obligations, and originating decision. It evidences authorization and is neither a policy rule nor the approval activity.

AuthorizationGrant.title: authorization grant payload
AuthorizationGrant.description: Authorization grant belongs to the authorization artifact category and differs from broader members in that it records an authorization result or credential-like grant issued after a policy decision, including grant scope, protected resource, subject actor, validity window, revocation state, and audit basis.

AuthorizationRequest.title: Authorization request payload
AuthorizationRequest.description: Authorization request is an information record whose defining condition is that it is an immutable information record binding subject, resource, action, scope, purpose, and time as input to one policy evaluation; it is not an authorization grant.

AuthorizationRevocation.title: Authorization revocation event payload
AuthorizationRevocation.description: Authorization revocation event is an event; its differentiating condition is that it records who terminated the future validity of an authorization grant, when, and under what authority; historical grant and performed-action evidence is retained.

CapabilityGrant.title: capability grant payload
CapabilityGrant.description: Capability grant is an authorization grant whose defining condition is that it records a grant for a specific tool, capability, resource surface, or operation so authorization can be checked at the point of invocation rather than inferred from actor identity alone.

DenyDecision.title: deny decision payload
DenyDecision.description: Deny decision is a policy decision whose defining condition is that it records a policy decision that prevents an operation, tool call, network request, disclosure, or side effect from proceeding and preserves the denial reason.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

EscalationDecision.title: escalation decision payload
EscalationDecision.description: Escalation decision belongs to the policy decision category and differs from broader members in that it records a policy decision that routes a request to a higher-authority reviewer, human approval, stricter sandbox, or additional verification path.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

HumanApproval.title: Human approval event payload
HumanApproval.description: Human approval event is an event whose defining condition is that it records an approval made by an authorized person for a specific request, scope, and validity interval; it is neither a reusable credential nor a subtype of policy decision.

PermissionPrompt.title: permission prompt payload
PermissionPrompt.description: Permission prompt is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it records a request for authority before an action proceeds, including requesting actor, operation, protected resource, scope, decision options, and approval channel.

PermissionResponse.title: permission response payload
PermissionResponse.description: As an information record, Permission response is distinguished by the fact that it records the response to a permission prompt, including allow, deny, escalate, conditionally allow, expiry, user or policy authority, and traceable rationale.

PermissionScope.title: permission scope payload
PermissionScope.description: Permission scope is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it bounds a single permission request or response by naming the requested operation, protected resource, actor, data zone, trust boundary, duration, and allowed side effects.

PolicyAction.title: policy action specification payload
PolicyAction.description: Policy action specification is a specification whose defining condition is that it describes the enforcement action chosen by a policy decision, such as allow, deny, redact, quarantine, require approval, route to sandbox, limit network egress, or escalate.

PolicyCondition.title: policy condition payload
PolicyCondition.description: Policy condition is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it states a safety policy predicate over subject actor, requested operation, protected resource, data zone, trust boundary, policy basis, runtime state, and enforcement mode.

PolicyDecision.title: policy decision payload
PolicyDecision.description: Policy decision is an information record whose defining condition is that it records the concrete allow, deny, escalate, redact, quarantine, sandbox, or approval-required outcome produced by evaluating a policy rule against a requested operation.

decision_kind.title: decision kind
decision_kind.description: Allow, deny, or escalate outcome produced by policy evaluation.

decision_id.title: policy-decision ID
decision_id.description: Stable identifier for one policy decision over subject, action, resource, and context.

reason.title: decision reason
reason.description: Explains how applicable rules, context, and evidence support the outcome.

decided_at.title: decided at
decided_at.description: UTC instant at which the policy decision took effect.

policy_version.title: policy version
policy_version.description: Pins the immutable policy version used for this decision.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject whose authority is evaluated.

action.title: action
action.description: Canonical action name evaluated by this policy decision.

resource_id.title: resource ID
resource_id.description: Identifies the tool, data, or system resource on which the action operates.

context_digest.title: context digest
context_digest.description: Pins environment, task, and risk context used by the decision without copying sensitive content.

issuer_id.title: issuer ID
issuer_id.description: Identifies the policy-enforcement actor issuing and owning the decision.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: Latest UTC instant until which enforcement may reuse this decision.

revoked_at.title: revoked at
revoked_at.description: UTC instant at which the decision was revoked early; null when not revoked.

PolicyEvaluation.title: Policy evaluation activity payload
PolicyEvaluation.description: As an activity, Policy evaluation activity is distinguished by the fact that it is a policy-evaluation activity judges one authorization request under a specified policy version, rules, conditions, and exceptions and produces a traceable allow, deny, or escalation decision.

PolicyException.title: policy exception payload
PolicyException.description: Policy exception is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it records a bounded exception to a policy rule with exception scope, authorized actor, protected resource, expiration, revocation condition, compensating control, and audit reason.

PolicyRule.title: policy rule payload
PolicyRule.description: As a specification, Policy rule is distinguished by the fact that it defines a safety or governance rule that can evaluate actor authority, requested operation, protected resource, data zone, trust boundary, runtime context, and enforcement action.

PolicySpecification.title: policy specification payload
PolicySpecification.description: Policy specification belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is an identity-bearing, versioned normative information object with an issuer and applicability scope. It uses conditions, rules, actions, and exceptions to prescribe permitted, required, or forbidden behavior by subjects over protected resources in a context; actual evaluations and decisions reference it by relation.

FilesystemPolicy.title: filesystem policy payload
FilesystemPolicy.description: Filesystem policy belongs to the isolation specification category and differs from broader members in that it defines filesystem read, write, execute, path, retention, and redaction constraints applied to sandbox commands, tool calls, side effects, and artifacts.

IsolationSpecification.title: isolation specification payload
IsolationSpecification.description: Isolation specification is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a versioned security specification with an applicable environment and enforcer. It states allow, deny, and monitoring constraints over processes, commands, paths, mounts, environment variables, network destinations, protocols, credentials, resource limits, and side effects. A concrete Sandbox references it through constrained_by.

ProcessPolicy.title: process policy payload
ProcessPolicy.description: Process policy is an isolation specification; its differentiating condition is that it defines process execution constraints such as command allowlist, argument limits, environment exposure, timeout, retry policy, resource limits, and sandbox escape handling.

Sandbox.title: sandbox payload
Sandbox.description: Sandbox is an entity; its differentiating condition is that it defines an isolated execution boundary that constrains process, filesystem, environment, network, credentials, and side effects for commands or tool calls.

SandboxEscapeRisk.title: sandbox escape risk payload
SandboxEscapeRisk.description: Sandbox escape risk belongs to the quality characteristic category and differs from broader members in that it records evidence that a command, process, file operation, network route, or tool result may bypass or weaken the intended sandbox isolation boundary.

AuthorityScope.title: authority scope payload
AuthorityScope.description: Authority scope is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it defines the canonical authorization envelope for an actor, tool, service, or runtime by naming permitted operations, protected resources, data zones, trust boundaries, expiry, and enforcement limits.

BoundaryCrossing.title: boundary crossing payload
BoundaryCrossing.description: As an event, Boundary crossing is distinguished by the fact that it captures an observable transfer of control, data, artifact, message, or authority across a trust boundary, carrying source actor, target actor, source zone, target zone, direction, transferred object, authority basis, policy decision, protocol or tool context, and trace evidence.

crossing_id.title: boundary-crossing ID
crossing_id.description: Stable identifier for one identity-bearing, auditable trust-boundary crossing.

direction.title: crossing direction
direction.description: Flow direction of the payload or action relative to source and target zones.

purpose.title: crossing purpose
purpose.description: Explicit business purpose used to assess necessity and least authority.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the crossing actually occurred.

payload_digest.title: payload digest
payload_digest.description: Immutable digest of the crossing payload or action parameters for audit without disclosure.

DataZone.title: data zone payload
DataZone.description: Data zone is a collection whose defining condition is that it groups data by trust, visibility, retention, and handling policy so that context, memory, tool output, and artifacts can carry boundary-aware controls.

ExternalBoundary.title: external boundary payload
ExternalBoundary.description: An ExternalBoundary is a TrustBoundary that separates the controlled agent runtime from outside organizations, services, users, or systems.

InternalBoundary.title: internal boundary payload
InternalBoundary.description: An InternalBoundary is a TrustBoundary that separates trusted runtime components, local stores, or internal services from less-trusted internal execution or data surfaces.

NetworkBoundary.title: network boundary payload
NetworkBoundary.description: A NetworkBoundary is a TrustBoundary at the transition between the runtime and network-accessible transports, proxies, resources, or remote services.

RemoteAgentBoundary.title: remote agent boundary payload
RemoteAgentBoundary.description: Remote agent boundary is a trust boundary; its differentiating condition is that it marks the opacity, identity, authority, and accountability trust boundary created when work, messages, tasks, or artifacts are delegated to or received from a remote agent.

ToolBoundary.title: tool boundary payload
ToolBoundary.description: A ToolBoundary is a TrustBoundary where model-controlled intent becomes an executable tool request or where tool output re-enters model-visible context.

TrustBoundary.title: trust boundary payload
TrustBoundary.description: Trust boundary is a specification whose defining condition is that it defines a boundary where identity, authority, visibility, execution control, data handling, or accountability assumptions change and therefore must be explicit in safety decisions.

AuditLog.title: audit log payload
AuditLog.description: Audit log belongs to the collection category and differs from broader members in that it is a reviewable collection or channel of log records, audit references, telemetry summaries, and disclosure-relevant evidence prepared for inspection, diagnostics, or export.

ErrorListener.title: error listener payload
ErrorListener.description: As a Log Consumer, Error listener is distinguished by the fact that it subscribes to runtime, sandbox, tool, protocol, or log events in order to detect and route errors for diagnosis.

EventSink.title: event sink payload
EventSink.description: Event sink is a Log Consumer; its differentiating condition is that it is a destination, consumer, receiver, or export target that accepts log streams, telemetry events, audit records, or trace exports for storage, monitoring, review, or forwarding.

LogArtifact.title: Log Artifact payload
LogArtifact.description: As an information record, Log Artifact is distinguished by the fact that it is a provenance-bearing information artifact formed from runtime, trace, or diagnostic evidence; individual LogRecord, composite TraceExport, and migrated ToolTranscript can specialize it.

LogConsumer.title: Log Consumer payload
LogConsumer.description: LogConsumer is a role consuming LogStreams, LogArtifacts, or TelemetryEvents to listen, route, store, or forward; a subscription configures a consumer and is not itself a consumer.

LogExportActivity.title: Log export activity payload
LogExportActivity.description: Log export activity belongs to the Telemetry export activity category and differs from broader members in that it reads records from a bounded log stream and forms a transportable batch.

LogListener.title: log listener payload
LogListener.description: As a Log Consumer, Log listener is distinguished by the fact that it is an observer component that subscribes to log streams or telemetry channels and routes records to diagnostics, review, metrics, audit logs, or event sinks.

LogRecord.title: log record payload
LogRecord.description: A LogRecord is a LogArtifact that stores a structured record derived from a trace, span, event, diagnostic message, warning, error, tool result, or audit-relevant observation.

log_record_id.title: log-record ID
log_record_id.description: Stable identifier for one immutable log record.

timestamp.title: timestamp
timestamp.description: UTC instant at which the logged event occurred or was observed.

severity.title: severity
severity.description: Controlled severity of the log record.

body.title: body
body.description: Log body after applicable redaction has been applied.

LogStream.title: log stream payload
LogStream.description: Log stream is a collection whose defining condition is that it is an ordered stream of log records or telemetry items delivered from runtime, tool, protocol, sandbox, or feedback components to listeners or sinks.

LogSubscription.title: log subscription payload
LogSubscription.description: Log subscription is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a subscription that registers a listener for a log stream, telemetry channel, audit feed, or diagnostic event source under filtering and retention conditions.

MeasurementReference.title: Measurement reference payload
MeasurementReference.description: Measurement reference belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is an immutable reference inside an export batch to a feedback-owned Measurement, retaining target identity, version, and digest without copying metric or measurement definitions.

reference_id.title: measurement reference
reference_id.description: This field uniquely identifies the measurement reference record.

MetricExportActivity.title: Measurement export activity payload
MetricExportActivity.description: Measurement export activity is a Telemetry export activity; its differentiating condition is that it references feedback-owned Measurement records and emits a batch of references without duplicating measurement semantics.

TelemetryExportActivity.title: Telemetry export activity payload
TelemetryExportActivity.description: Telemetry export activity is an activity whose defining condition is that it reads runtime observations, logs, or measurements and creates a verifiable immutable export batch under a fixed scope and format; ownership of original events and measurements does not move with export.

TelemetryExportBatch.title: Telemetry export batch payload
TelemetryExportBatch.description: Telemetry export batch is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is an immutable information collection produced by one export activity, fixing batch identity, format, time range, source fingerprint, and member order.

batch_id.title: batch
batch_id.description: This field uniquely identifies the batch record.

ToolTranscript.title: Tool Transcript payload
ToolTranscript.description: A ToolTranscript is a LogArtifact that preserves the ordered record of tool requests, permission prompts, ToolCallAttempt executions, results, warnings, and follow-up observations.

TraceExport.title: trace export payload
TraceExport.description: Trace export belongs to the Telemetry export batch category and differs from broader members in that it is an exported trace package or portable trace artifact containing selected trace records, spans, events, attributes, redaction state, and provenance for debugging or review.

batch_id.title: batch
batch_id.description: This field uniquely identifies the batch record.

TraceExportActivity.title: Trace export activity payload
TraceExportActivity.description: Trace export activity is a Telemetry export activity whose defining condition is that it selects and packages TraceRecord and span evidence.

CostMetric.title: cost metric payload
CostMetric.description: Cost metric belongs to the metric category and differs from broader members in that it is a metric dimension measuring token, compute, money, call-count, storage, or operational cost for a run, task, tool call, route, or evaluation episode.

EvaluationCriterion.title: evaluation criterion payload
EvaluationCriterion.description: Evaluation criterion is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a generic feedback-domain criterion that defines the scoring, success, rubric, or metric condition used to assess agent behavior while benchmark-specific criteria remain adapter mappings.

EvaluationRun.title: evaluation run payload
EvaluationRun.description: Evaluation run belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is an assessment episode that applies metrics, rubric criteria, success criteria, and scoring logic to observable agent behavior, run attempts, tasks, artifacts, or tool use.

evaluation_run_id.title: evaluation-run ID
evaluation_run_id.description: Stable identifier for one evaluation run over a pinned subject version.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which the evaluation run started.

evaluator_id.title: evaluator ID
evaluator_id.description: Identifies the actor or system that performed or owned the evaluation.

status.title: evaluation status
status.description: Queued, active, completed, failed, or cancelled state of the evaluation run.

subject_id.title: subject ID
subject_id.description: Identifies the subject evaluated by this run.

subject_version.title: subject version
subject_version.description: Pins the immutable version or content digest of the evaluated subject.

EvaluationScenario.title: evaluation scenario payload
EvaluationScenario.description: As a specification, Evaluation scenario is distinguished by the fact that it is a generic evaluation setup describing task conditions, inputs, constraints, and expected observations while adapter-specific benchmark scenario fields remain outside feedback core.

EvaluationSpecification.title: Evaluation Specification payload
EvaluationSpecification.description: Evaluation Specification belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is a static, versionable specification used by evaluation runs, covering scenarios, rubrics, and criteria; it stores neither the evaluation execution nor observed measurements.

LatencyMetric.title: latency metric payload
LatencyMetric.description: As a metric, Latency metric is distinguished by the fact that it is a metric dimension measuring elapsed time, step latency, tool-call duration, queue delay, or response timing for observable agent work.

Measurement.title: Measurement payload
Measurement.description: A Measurement is an information record that references a Metric definition, measured subject, EvaluationRun, unit, value, time, confidence, and provenance; the Metric is reusable while each observed value has independent identity.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: captures confidence as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

Metric.title: metric payload
Metric.description: As a specification, Metric is distinguished by the fact that it is a measurable property of a run, task, tool call, route, artifact, policy decision, or feedback loop, expressed independently of benchmark-specific scoring schemes.

RobustnessMetric.title: robustness metric payload
RobustnessMetric.description: As a metric, Robustness metric is distinguished by the fact that it is a metric dimension measuring stability under retries, perturbations, long-horizon tasks, tool failures, context changes, or adversarial pressure.

Rubric.title: rubric payload
Rubric.description: Rubric is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a generic scoring guide that defines criteria, weights, thresholds, and interpretation rules for evaluation without embedding benchmark-specific leaderboard semantics.

SafetyMetric.title: safety metric payload
SafetyMetric.description: As a metric, Safety metric is distinguished by the fact that it is a metric dimension measuring policy compliance, risk exposure, unsafe output rate, boundary violations, or safety review outcomes without owning enforcement decisions.

Score.title: score payload
Score.description: As a Measurement, Score is distinguished by the fact that it is a numeric, ordinal, categorical, or pass-fail assessment value produced for a metric or rubric during an evaluation run.

confidence.title: Confidence
confidence.description: captures confidence as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

value.title: Score value
value.description: captures score as a number scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

SuccessCriterion.title: success criterion payload
SuccessCriterion.description: As an evaluation criterion, Success criterion is distinguished by the fact that it is a condition used to decide whether a task, run, artifact, recovery attempt, or evaluation objective is acceptable under the relevant rubric or policy.

ChangeProposal.title: Change proposal payload
ChangeProposal.description: Change proposal is an information record whose defining condition is that it is an auditable modification recommendation derived from learning signals and awaiting governance approval; it states target, rationale, expected impact, and validation conditions without directly mutating runtime configuration.

FeedbackLearningSignal.title: Feedback learning signal payload
FeedbackLearningSignal.description: Feedback learning signal is a learning signal; its differentiating condition is that it is derived from explicit human or system feedback and retains its source and applicability context.

LearningSignal.title: learning signal payload
LearningSignal.description: Learning signal is an information record whose defining condition is that it is traceable information normalized from feedback, reward, or preference evidence for an optimization loop to evaluate change proposals; it does not execute a change itself.

OptimizationLoop.title: optimization loop payload
OptimizationLoop.description: Optimization loop belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is a bounded feedback loop that consumes feedback, metrics, scores, review findings, or learning signals to improve later routing, tool selection, prompts, policy thresholds, or task attempts.

OptimizationTarget.title: optimization target payload
OptimizationTarget.description: Optimization target is a specification whose defining condition is that it specifies the property and direction to improve and is referenced by reviews and measurements.

ParameterChangeProposal.title: Parameter change proposal payload
ParameterChangeProposal.description: Parameter change proposal is a Change proposal; its differentiating condition is that it recommends changes only to parameter values with explicit bounds and validation metrics.

PolicyChangeProposal.title: Policy change proposal payload
PolicyChangeProposal.description: Policy change proposal is a Change proposal whose defining condition is that it recommends changes to governance rules, conditions, or decision thresholds and must pass a policy decision.

PreferenceLearningSignal.title: Preference learning signal payload
PreferenceLearningSignal.description: Preference learning signal belongs to the learning signal category and differs from broader members in that it is formed from a traceable comparison or ranking judgment among candidates.

PromptChangeProposal.title: Prompt change proposal payload
PromptChangeProposal.description: As a Change proposal, Prompt change proposal is distinguished by the fact that it recommends a prompt-template or instruction change while retaining target version, evidence, and evaluation conditions.

RewardLearningSignal.title: Reward learning signal payload
RewardLearningSignal.description: Reward learning signal is a learning signal; its differentiating condition is that it is formed from reward values for one or more runs together with their computation context.

AutomatedReview.title: automated review payload
AutomatedReview.description: Automated review is a Review Activity whose defining condition is that it is a machine-produced review pass over a task, output, plan, trace, or artifact that records findings, confidence, criteria, and provenance without replacing human approval when policy requires it.

Correction.title: correction payload
Correction.description: As a Correction Activity, Correction is distinguished by the fact that it is a change proposed or applied to an artifact, plan, output, route, context assembly, or tool-use choice in response to feedback, diagnostics, failed validation, or review findings.

CorrectionActivity.title: Correction Activity payload
CorrectionActivity.description: Correction Activity belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is triggered by feedback or failure evidence and modifies, recovers, or rolls back an artifact, plan, state, or external effect; it is constrained by a plan and should be reevaluated.

CritiqueArtifact.title: critique artifact payload
CritiqueArtifact.description: As a revision artifact, Critique artifact is distinguished by the fact that it is an observable critique result produced by a review or evaluator step, including findings, requested changes, confidence, and provenance.

Feedback.title: feedback payload
Feedback.description: Feedback belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is an interpreted signal returned from review, diagnostics, metrics, user judgment, policy checks, or runtime evidence to improve, revise, block, approve, or reprioritize later work.

FeedbackEvent.title: feedback event payload
FeedbackEvent.description: Feedback event is an event; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable control-loop event that returns critique, warning, preference, correction, approval, or revision signal to a task, route, worker, or optimizer.

HumanReview.title: human review payload
HumanReview.description: Human review is a Review Activity; its differentiating condition is that it is a review evidence supplied by a human actor, including approval, requested changes, blocking concerns, quality judgment, accountable reviewer identity, and reviewed object.

RecoveryAction.title: recovery action payload
RecoveryAction.description: Recovery action belongs to the Correction Activity category and differs from broader members in that it is a feedback-triggered or failure-triggered action such as repair, retry, reroute, stop, escalate, rollback, or resume that responds to a warning, denial, failed attempt, or review finding.

RecoveryPlan.title: recovery plan payload
RecoveryPlan.description: Recovery plan is a specification whose defining condition is that it is a plan that selects one or more recovery actions, links them to the failure or feedback evidence that triggered them, and bounds retry, rollback, escalation, or stop behavior.

ReflectionRecord.title: reflection record payload
ReflectionRecord.description: Reflection record is a revision artifact whose defining condition is that it is an observable review summary or rationale artifact used for improvement while excluding private reasoning transcripts.

Review.title: review payload
Review.description: Review is an information record whose defining condition is that it is a bounded inspection record over a task, artifact, trace segment, plan, answer, tool result, or policy-sensitive decision that can produce findings, approvals, requested changes, or feedback.

review_id.title: review ID
review_id.description: Stable identifier for the review record produced by one review activity.

subject_version.title: subject version
subject_version.description: Pins the immutable subject version examined by the review.

status.title: review status
status.description: Active, completed, withdrawn, or superseded state of the review record.

completed_at.title: completed at
completed_at.description: UTC instant at which review completed; null while active.

ReviewActivity.title: Review Activity payload
ReviewActivity.description: Review Activity is an activity; its differentiating condition is that it is an inspection with reviewer, assignment, subject version, criteria, and time bounds; it produces Review records and findings rather than being identical to them.

ReviewEvent.title: review event payload
ReviewEvent.description: As an event, Review event is distinguished by the fact that it is an observable control-loop event that requests or performs review of an output, state, plan, safety condition, evidence bundle, or completion claim.

ReviewFinding.title: review finding payload
ReviewFinding.description: Review finding belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is a specific issue, observation, recommendation, defect, or approval note discovered during review and linked to the artifact, trace event, tool result, plan, or decision it concerns.

RevisionArtifact.title: revision artifact payload
RevisionArtifact.description: Revision artifact belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is information produced or used by review or optimization to state findings, improvement grounds, proposed changes, or stop/retry lineage and traceable to the evaluated target and source evidence.

RevisionPlan.title: revision plan payload
RevisionPlan.description: Revision plan is a specification whose defining condition is that it is a planned remediation path that states what to change, which prior attempt it revises, and which criterion or feedback triggered the revision.

RollbackAction.title: rollback action payload
RollbackAction.description: As a Correction Activity, Rollback action is distinguished by the fact that it is an action that reverses, compensates, or invalidates an unsafe side effect, state transition, artifact revision, memory update, route choice, or external operation under audit control.

BlockingError.title: blocking error payload
BlockingError.description: Blocking error is a failure mode; its differentiating condition is that it is a failure condition that blocks or terminates a run attempt, tool invocation, policy-gated operation, or protocol exchange until recovery, escalation, rollback, or operator intervention occurs.

ConfidenceSignal.title: confidence signal payload
ConfidenceSignal.description: As a Diagnostic Signal, Confidence signal is distinguished by the fact that it is a confidence or uncertainty evidence attached to an observation, review finding, metric, retrieval result, answer, route choice, or policy-relevant decision.

DiagnosticMessage.title: diagnostic message payload
DiagnosticMessage.description: Diagnostic message is a Diagnostic Signal whose defining condition is that it is a diagnostic output, debug message, explanation, or remediation hint derived from logs, trace events, validation failures, warnings, or review findings.

DiagnosticSignal.title: Diagnostic Signal payload
DiagnosticSignal.description: Diagnostic Signal is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is information attached to a run, attempt, result, or review to express warning, risk, confidence, or diagnosis; an event may emit it, but it is not the occurrence itself.

ErrorEvent.title: error event payload
ErrorEvent.description: As a Failure Event, Error event is distinguished by the fact that it is an observable event recording a failure, exception, timeout, validation error, blocked operation, or unrecoverable runtime condition with trace and diagnostic context.

error_code.title: error code
error_code.description: Machine-readable code for stable error classification and aggregation.

error_event_id.title: error-event ID
error_event_id.description: Stable identifier for one error event that actually occurred.

severity.title: severity
severity.description: Controlled level expressing the impact of the error.

retryable.title: retryable
retryable.description: Whether creating a new attempt under equivalent conditions is an allowed recovery strategy.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the error actually occurred.

ErrorStream.title: error stream payload
ErrorStream.description: Error stream is a collection whose defining condition is that it is an ordered stream of error events, blocking failures, retryable failures, warning escalations, and diagnostic messages observed during runtime or protocol execution.

FailureEvent.title: Failure Event payload
FailureEvent.description: Failure Event is an event whose defining condition is that it is an abnormal, failed, or warning occurrence at an explicit run, call, protocol, or policy location, with time, location, and impact; a failure mode classifies it and diagnostic signals explain it.

FailureMode.title: failure mode payload
FailureMode.description: Failure mode belongs to the state category and differs from broader members in that it classifies a recurring way an agent run, tool invocation, retrieval, policy gate, or protocol interaction can fail.

RetryableError.title: retryable error payload
RetryableError.description: As a failure mode, Retryable error is distinguished by the fact that it identifies an error that may be retried under bounded policy because the failure is transient, recoverable, or externally caused.

RiskSignal.title: risk signal payload
RiskSignal.description: Risk signal is a Diagnostic Signal; its differentiating condition is that it is a safety bridge signal recording evidence that an action, output, tool result, boundary crossing, instruction, or memory item may be policy-sensitive, unsafe, or trust-boundary relevant.

Warning.title: warning payload
Warning.description: A Warning is a DiagnosticSignal that records degraded confidence, policy concern, risky context, partial execution, or a recoverable runtime issue as a non-fatal observable signal without claiming final failure.

warning_code.title: Warning code
warning_code.description: captures warning code as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

WarningEvent.title: warning event payload
WarningEvent.description: As a Failure Event, Warning event is distinguished by the fact that it is an observable event that raises a warning from runtime, tool, policy, retrieval, review, or telemetry evidence while execution may continue under constraints.

Chunk.title: chunk payload
Chunk.description: Chunk belongs to the chunk artifact category and differs from broader members in that it is a bounded segment of a document, output, message, or memory item with source span, boundary, provenance, and retrieval metadata used for indexing and context assembly.

chunk_id.title: chunk ID
chunk_id.description: Stable identifier for the chunk within a source-document version.

ordinal.title: ordinal
ordinal.description: Zero-based order of the chunk in its source document.

ChunkArtifact.title: chunk artifact payload
ChunkArtifact.description: Chunk artifact is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is a provenance-, version-, and span-bearing information artifact that carries a Chunk, a locally grounded SituatedChunk, or a ChunkReference to an exact chunk and source span. It excludes chunking runs, boundary parameters, and standalone quality judgments.

ChunkBoundary.title: chunk boundary payload
ChunkBoundary.description: structural boundary for a chunk, such as byte offset, token span, line range, page range, section break, semantic boundary, or overlap boundary.

ChunkCompression.title: chunk compression activity payload
ChunkCompression.description: Chunk compression activity is a chunk processing activity; its differentiating condition is that it is a ChunkProcessingActivity that compresses one or more chunks while preserving source anchors and produces a new Chunk; the compressed output remains a chunk rather than the activity itself.

ChunkContextNote.title: chunk context note payload
ChunkContextNote.description: short note attached to a chunk that explains its local section, surrounding context, or reason for retrieval relevance.

ChunkingRun.title: chunking run payload
ChunkingRun.description: As a chunk processing activity, Chunking run is distinguished by the fact that it is an observable event that divides a document, output, or memory item into retrievable chunks with boundary, overlap, span, and provenance metadata.

ChunkMetadata.title: chunk metadata payload
ChunkMetadata.description: Chunk metadata is an information record whose defining condition is that it is auditable information attached to a Chunk or ChunkingRun with an explicit metadata kind, value or evidence, and provenance. It includes ChunkBoundary, ChunkOverlap, ChunkProvenance, ChunkQualitySignal, and ChunkContextNote. It is neither the Chunk itself nor an activity changing it.

boundary_start.title: boundary start
boundary_start.description: The chunk's starting offset in its normalized source.

boundary_end.title: boundary end
boundary_end.description: The chunk's ending offset in its normalized source.

overlap_size.title: overlap size
overlap_size.description: The number of tokens or characters shared with an adjacent chunk.

quality_score.title: quality score
quality_score.description: A chunk-quality value computed under an explicit scoring specification.

context_note.title: context note
context_note.description: A short note without independent identity that explains the chunk's context.

ChunkOverlap.title: chunk overlap payload
ChunkOverlap.description: overlap span shared between adjacent chunks to preserve local continuity during retrieval and context assembly.

ChunkProcessingActivity.title: chunk processing activity payload
ChunkProcessingActivity.description: A ChunkProcessingActivity is an activity with run identity, input version, strategy or model, output version, time, result, and error. It covers ChunkingRun, SituatingPromptRun, and ChunkCompression. Its products point to Chunk or SituatedChunk through produces; the activity itself is not an information artifact.

ChunkProvenance.title: chunk provenance payload
ChunkProvenance.description: Chunk provenance is a chunk metadata whose defining condition is that it is a provenance record linking a chunk to its document, source span, version, checksum, ingestion run, and transformation history.

boundary_start.title: boundary start
boundary_start.description: The chunk's starting offset in its normalized source.

boundary_end.title: boundary end
boundary_end.description: The chunk's ending offset in its normalized source.

overlap_size.title: overlap size
overlap_size.description: The number of tokens or characters shared with an adjacent chunk.

quality_score.title: quality score
quality_score.description: A chunk-quality value computed under an explicit scoring specification.

context_note.title: context note
context_note.description: A short note without independent identity that explains the chunk's context.

ChunkQualitySignal.title: chunk quality signal payload
ChunkQualitySignal.description: quality signal for a chunk such as extraction confidence, parsing completeness, duplication status, staleness, toxicity, or retrieval usefulness.

ChunkReference.title: chunk reference payload
ChunkReference.description: As an information record, Chunk reference is distinguished by the fact that it is a reference from a chunk or retrieved chunk to the exact source span, anchor, line range, page range, offset, or document version it came from.

SituatedChunk.title: situated chunk payload
SituatedChunk.description: As a chunk, Situated chunk is distinguished by the fact that it is a chunk augmented with local document context, section explanation, source purpose, or task-relevant grounding note before indexing.

chunk_id.title: chunk ID
chunk_id.description: Stable identifier for the chunk within a source-document version.

ordinal.title: ordinal
ordinal.description: Zero-based order of the chunk in its source document.

SituatingPromptRun.title: situating prompt run payload
SituatingPromptRun.description: Situating prompt run is a chunk processing activity; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable event that adds document-local or task-local context to a chunk so retrieval can preserve section meaning, source purpose, and surrounding evidence.

ContextArtifact.title: context artifact payload
ContextArtifact.description: Context artifact is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is information with context version, provenance, budget placement, and consumer association. It covers ContextWindow, ContextSummary, ContextSlot, and ContextSource. Final ContextPackage is uniquely owned by this module and referenced by other modules through canonical relations; ContextAssembly and refresh are activities or events, and rules are excluded.

ContextAssembly.title: context assembly payload
ContextAssembly.description: Context assembly belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is an observable activity selecting, ordering, budgeting, and excluding information and producing a window and ContextPackage by relation.

assembly_id.title: context-assembly ID
assembly_id.description: Stable identifier for one context selection, ordering, and budgeting activity.

context_version.title: context version
context_version.description: Identifies the immutable context-snapshot version produced by this activity.

ContextBudget.title: context budget payload
ContextBudget.description: As a context rule, Context budget is distinguished by the fact that it defines the token, window, cost, freshness, and priority limits used when deciding what information may enter a runtime context.

ContextExclusion.title: context exclusion decision payload
ContextExclusion.description: Context exclusion decision is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it records one decision to omit material for privacy, trust, relevance, safety, or budget and is not a subtype of a reusable rule.

ContextOrderingActivity.title: Context ordering activity payload
ContextOrderingActivity.description: As an activity, Context ordering activity is distinguished by the fact that it determines a stable order and adjacency among selected items.

ContextOrderingRule.title: context ordering rule payload
ContextOrderingRule.description: Context ordering rule is a context rule; its differentiating condition is that it is a rule that orders context by priority, recency, freshness, authority, source trust, retrieval rank, task relevance, message role, and budget pressure.

ContextPackage.title: context package payload
ContextPackage.description: Context package belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it is a bundles the exact message envelopes, instructions, content blocks, source references, retrieval candidates, tool observations, citations, exclusions, and budget metadata visible to one model call or agent step.

package_id.title: context package ID
package_id.description: Uniquely identifies a versioned context-package lineage within one system.

version.title: version
version.description: Identifies one immutable version within a context-package lineage.

content_digest.title: content digest
content_digest.description: An integrity digest computed over the ordered content and material metadata of this version.

assembled_at.title: assembled at
assembled_at.description: Records when this immutable context version finished assembly.

ContextRefreshEvent.title: context refresh event payload
ContextRefreshEvent.description: Context refresh event is an event whose defining condition is that it is an observable update that reruns retrieval, replaces stale memory, recomputes summaries, or reorders context after new evidence, feedback, or state changes.

ContextRule.title: context rule payload
ContextRule.description: A ContextRule is a specification with identity, version, applicability scope, priority, and evaluation conditions. It covers ContextOrderingRule and ContextBudget. A ContextExclusion is a decision resulting from applying rules and is recorded by relation rather than subclassing the rule.

ContextSelectionActivity.title: Context selection activity payload
ContextSelectionActivity.description: As an activity, Context selection activity is distinguished by the fact that it is a context-selection activity chooses candidates eligible for context using query relevance, authorization, and budget.

ContextSlot.title: context slot payload
ContextSlot.description: Context slot is an information record; its differentiating condition is that it is a reserved position or segment in a context window for system instruction, task state, retrieved evidence, memory record, summary, tool result, or artifact.

ContextSource.title: context source payload
ContextSource.description: Context source is a source reference whose defining condition is that it is an origin descriptor for context content, such as message, memory record, retrieval result, document chunk, tool result, trace observation, or artifact.

uri.title: Resource URI
uri.description: captures URI as a string scalar on agent-system artifacts, resources, events, policies, or adapter records.

reference_id.title: reference ID
reference_id.description: Stable identifier for this reference record, not for the referent itself.

canonical_uri.title: canonical URI
canonical_uri.description: Canonical URI locating the referenced resource when available.

ContextSummary.title: context summary payload
ContextSummary.description: Context summary is a context artifact; its differentiating condition is that it is a compressed representation of prior state, evidence, conversation, retrieved material, or memory records used to fit a context budget without storing hidden reasoning.

ContextTrimmingActivity.title: Context trimming activity payload
ContextTrimmingActivity.description: Context trimming activity belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is a context-trimming activity removes or shortens ordered content to fit the window budget without changing source identity.

ContextWindow.title: context window payload
ContextWindow.description: Context window belongs to the context artifact category and differs from broader members in that it is the bounded set of instructions, messages, retrieved chunks, memory records, summaries, tool results, and artifacts visible to one model call or agent step.

VisibleContextWindow.title: visible context window payload
VisibleContextWindow.description: Visible context window is an information record; its differentiating condition is that VisibleContextWindow denotes the actual context snapshot visible after selection and trimming, not a window-selection specification; OutputWindow retains the specification semantics.

DenseVector.title: dense vector payload
DenseVector.description: Dense vector is a vector representation whose defining condition is that it is a dense embedding vector representation produced by an embedding model for semantic similarity search over memory records, chunks, or context candidates.

Embedding.title: embedding payload
Embedding.description: Embedding is a representation; its differentiating condition is that it records a dense, sparse, lexical, or graph representation used by memory and retrieval indexes to compare or retrieve source material.

EmbeddingRun.title: embedding run payload
EmbeddingRun.description: As an index activity, Embedding run is distinguished by the fact that it is an observable event in which a specific embedding model and version encodes a chunk, document span, memory record, or query and produces an EmbeddingVector.

EmbeddingVector.title: embedding vector payload
EmbeddingVector.description: Embedding vector is a vector representation whose defining condition is that it is a vector representation produced from a chunk, document span, memory record, or query for similarity search, ranking, clustering, or index construction.

GraphEmbedding.title: graph embedding payload
GraphEmbedding.description: Graph embedding belongs to the embedding category and differs from broader members in that it records a graph-derived representation used to retrieve or rank graph nodes, edges, neighborhoods, or structured context candidates.

HybridIndex.title: hybrid index payload
HybridIndex.description: Hybrid index is an index whose defining condition is that it is a retrieval index that combines dense semantic vectors, sparse lexical features, or graph/context signals before scoring or rank fusion.

Index.title: index payload
Index.description: An Index is an entity that provides a versioned retrieval structure with a retrieval kind, configuration identity, and input-representation requirements; IndexVersion identifies each build, part_of connects shards and entries, and hosts connects backend databases.

IndexActivity.title: index activity payload
IndexActivity.description: An IndexActivity is an activity with run identity, source input, model or index configuration, output version, result, and error. It covers EmbeddingRun and IndexBuildRun. IndexRefreshEvent remains an independent event connected to versions through supersedes.

IndexBuildRun.title: index build run payload
IndexBuildRun.description: Index build run belongs to the index activity category and differs from broader members in that it is an observable event that builds or refreshes an index from memory records, chunks, embeddings, lexical features, or graph links and emits an IndexVersion.

IndexEntry.title: index entry payload
IndexEntry.description: Index entry is an information record whose defining condition is that it records an addressable item inside a retrieval index, including key, source reference, representation, and index-version metadata.

IndexKey.title: index key payload
IndexKey.description: Index key belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it records the key or lookup handle used to retrieve an index entry or candidate set from a memory or discovery index.

IndexRefreshEvent.title: index refresh event payload
IndexRefreshEvent.description: Index refresh event belongs to the event category and differs from broader members in that it records an observable memory-index update, rebuild, or refresh event and the index version it produces.

IndexRefreshRun.title: Index refresh run payload
IndexRefreshRun.description: As an index activity, Index refresh run is distinguished by the fact that it is an activity that reads an existing index version and new representations, rebuilds affected entries, and produces a new immutable index version.

IndexShard.title: index shard payload
IndexShard.description: Index shard is an entity whose defining condition is that it is a partition of a vector, lexical, hybrid, or graph index used to scale retrieval while preserving index-version provenance.

IndexVersion.title: index version payload
IndexVersion.description: Index version is an information record whose defining condition is that it is a versioned snapshot of an index with build provenance, source coverage, embedding model, lexical configuration, shard layout, and validity period.

index_id.title: index ID
index_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical index across versions.

version.title: index version
version.description: Version value identifying one immutable index snapshot.

built_at.title: built at
built_at.description: UTC instant at which this index version finished building.

Representation.title: representation payload
Representation.description: A Representation is an information record that retains an encoding method, generation provenance, version, and represented object and may use an Embedding or another vector or structured form; it is distinct from its generating activity, hosting index, and target Chunk or MemoryRecord.

SparseVector.title: sparse vector payload
SparseVector.description: A SparseVector is a VectorRepresentation using weighted token, term, or feature dimensions for lexical or hybrid retrieval over memory records and chunks.

TextEmbedding.title: text embedding payload
TextEmbedding.description: As an embedding, Text embedding is distinguished by the fact that it records a text-derived representation used by retrieval indexes for similarity search, ranking, clustering, or context selection.

TfIdfIndex.title: TF-IDF index payload
TfIdfIndex.description: TF-IDF index belongs to the index category and differs from broader members in that it is a lexical index that represents documents, chunks, or memory records through term frequency and inverse document frequency weights.

VectorDatabase.title: vector database payload
VectorDatabase.description: As a memory store, Vector database is distinguished by the fact that it is a storage and query surface for vector indexes, embeddings, metadata filters, and nearest-neighbor retrieval over memory records or chunks.

deployment_scope.title: deployment scope
deployment_scope.description: States whether the store endpoint is deployed locally, remotely, or in a hybrid arrangement; the facet does not change MemoryStore identity.

VectorIndex.title: vector index payload
VectorIndex.description: Vector index is an index; its differentiating condition is that it is an index structure that organizes embedding vectors and metadata so retrieval queries can return similar memory records, chunks, or context candidates.

VectorRepresentation.title: vector representation payload
VectorRepresentation.description: A VectorRepresentation is a Representation distinguished by vector dimensions, numeric type, sparsity, distance compatibility, and generation version. It covers DenseVector, SparseVector, and EmbeddingVector and is not a VectorIndex or VectorDatabase.

Database.title: database payload
Database.description: A Database is an ingestible resource that represents a data-store surface whose rows or query results can be located through source references and used as context evidence.

DatabaseRow.title: database row payload
DatabaseRow.description: A DatabaseRow is ingestible information representing a database row or query-result row that can be addressed through a source reference subject to policy and provenance.

DatabaseTable.title: database table payload
DatabaseTable.description: Database table belongs to the ingestible resource category and differs from broader members in that it represents a structured table within a database resource surface whose rows or slices may be referenced as context evidence.

DeduplicationActivity.title: Deduplication activity payload
DeduplicationActivity.description: Deduplication activity is an activity whose defining condition is that it compares normalized content, provenance, and policy scope, prevents duplicate memory writes, and produces an auditable deduplication event.

DeduplicationEvent.title: deduplication event payload
DeduplicationEvent.description: Deduplication event is an event; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable ingestion event that detects duplicate or near-duplicate source material and links the retained record to the suppressed duplicate evidence.

DirectoryResource.title: directory resource payload
DirectoryResource.description: Directory resource belongs to the ingestible resource category and differs from broader members in that it represents a directory-like resource that scopes files, paths, discovery, permissions, and traversal policy.

Document.title: document payload
Document.description: Document is an Ingestible information; its differentiating condition is that it is a memory source artifact loaded or attached for parsing, metadata capture, chunking, indexing, citation, or later retrieval.

DocumentLoader.title: document loader payload
DocumentLoader.description: DocumentLoader is the capability to obtain resource content under access and trust controls; it relates to IngestionPolicy through use rather than a false common parent.

DocumentMetadata.title: document metadata payload
DocumentMetadata.description: As an information record, Document metadata is distinguished by the fact that it is a metadata record describing a document's source, version, checksum, author, timestamp, format, permissions, trust zone, and parsing state for memory use.

FileResource.title: file resource payload
FileResource.description: As an ingestible resource, File resource is distinguished by the fact that it represents a file-like resource with path, version, digest, permissions, and possible source spans available through a resource surface.

FileSystem.title: file system payload
FileSystem.description: File system belongs to the entity category and differs from broader members in that it represents a filesystem resource surface governed by runtime, safety, and access policy rather than a context input artifact.

GraphEdge.title: graph edge payload
GraphEdge.description: Graph edge is an Ingestible information; its differentiating condition is that it represents an addressable relationship in a graph store connecting graph nodes with labels, direction, provenance, and context relevance.

GraphNode.title: graph node payload
GraphNode.description: As an Ingestible information, Graph node is distinguished by the fact that it represents an addressable node in a graph store that may carry properties, labels, provenance, and context-relevant references.

GraphStore.title: graph store payload
GraphStore.description: Graph store is an ingestible resource; its differentiating condition is that it represents a graph storage or graph retrieval surface whose nodes and edges may be referenced without making the store itself a context artifact.

IngestibleCollection.title: Ingestible collection payload
IngestibleCollection.description: Ingestible collection is a collection whose defining condition is that it is an information collection entering memory ingestion as one ordered or unordered whole.

IngestibleInformation.title: Ingestible information payload
IngestibleInformation.description: As an information record, Ingestible information is distinguished by the fact that it is an information object that a memory-ingestion activity can parse, validate, and retain with provenance.

IngestibleResource.title: ingestible resource payload
IngestibleResource.description: An IngestibleResource is an entity or information resource with stable identity or location plus access and trust context. An IngestionRun may read it through a DocumentLoader and transform it into a document, metadata, chunks, or memory records. It describes ingestion input only and excludes tool capability resources, runtime activities, and ingestion audit events.

IngestionPolicy.title: ingestion policy payload
IngestionPolicy.description: As a specification, Ingestion policy is distinguished by the fact that it is a policy that decides which sources may be loaded, parsed, deduplicated, attached, stored, excluded, or sent for safety review before memory persistence.

IngestionRun.title: ingestion run payload
IngestionRun.description: Ingestion run belongs to the activity category and differs from broader members in that it is an observable activity with inputs, loader, policy, result, and error, not a resource or artifact.

ingestion_run_id.title: ingestion-run ID
ingestion_run_id.description: Stable identifier for one source-to-memory ingestion run.

started_at.title: started at
started_at.description: UTC instant at which ingestion started.

NormalizationActivity.title: Normalization activity payload
NormalizationActivity.description: As an activity, Normalization activity is distinguished by the fact that it converts ingestible information into a document with consistent encoding, structure, and provenance fields without changing source identity.

SourceAttachment.title: source attachment payload
SourceAttachment.description: Source attachment is an Ingestible information whose defining condition is that it is an attached source object or reference carried into ingestion with provenance, location, checksum, and trust-boundary metadata.

StorageArea.title: storage area payload
StorageArea.description: Storage area is an entity whose defining condition is that it represents a bounded storage surface or namespace governed by memory, runtime, access, and trust policies.

TextCorpus.title: text corpus payload
TextCorpus.description: Text corpus belongs to the Ingestible collection category and differs from broader members in that it represents a collection of text documents or text-bearing records prepared for ingestion, retrieval, citation, or context assembly.

TextDocument.title: text document payload
TextDocument.description: As an information record, Text document is distinguished by the fact that it represents a text-bearing information source that can be referenced, chunked, indexed, cited, or assembled into context with source provenance.

MemoryAuditEvent.title: memory audit event payload
MemoryAuditEvent.description: Memory audit event is an event whose defining condition is that it is an observable audit event that records who read, wrote, updated, validated, deleted, exported, or reused a memory record.

MemoryCompaction.title: memory compaction payload
MemoryCompaction.description: Memory compaction is a memory operation whose defining condition is that it is an operation that compresses memory content or history to reduce storage and context cost while preserving source anchors and retrieval utility.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Stable identifier for one memory-lifecycle operation.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the operation took effect.

reason.title: operation reason
reason.description: Auditable reason for performing the lifecycle operation.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Identifies the actor that initiated or performed the memory operation.

policy_id.title: policy ID
policy_id.description: Identifies the policy version authorizing and constraining the operation.

input_version.title: input version
input_version.description: Memory-record version read by the operation.

output_version.title: output version
output_version.description: New memory-record version produced by the operation; deletion is represented by a tombstone version.

result_status.title: result status
result_status.description: Controlled succeeded, failed, conflicted, or skipped result of the operation.

error_code.title: error code
error_code.description: Machine-readable error code when the operation fails; null on success.

MemoryConflict.title: memory conflict payload
MemoryConflict.description: Memory conflict is a state; its differentiating condition is that it is a record of contradiction between memory records, source evidence, preferences, policies, or current user instructions that requires resolution or supersession.

MemoryConsolidation.title: memory consolidation payload
MemoryConsolidation.description: Memory consolidation is a memory operation; its differentiating condition is that it is an observable operation that distills multiple observations, episodes, records, or feedback items into a summary, semantic memory, reflective memory, or procedure.

operation_id.title: operation ID
operation_id.description: Stable identifier for one memory-lifecycle operation.

occurred_at.title: occurred at
occurred_at.description: UTC instant at which the operation took effect.

reason.title: operation reason
reason.description: Auditable reason for performing the lifecycle operation.

actor_id.title: actor ID
actor_id.description: Identifies the actor that initiated or performed the memory operation.

policy_id.title: policy ID
policy_id.description: Identifies the policy version authorizing and constraining the operation.

input_version.title: input version
input_version.description: Memory-record version read by the operation.

output_version.title: output version

MemoryOperation.description: A MemoryOperation is an activity with operation identity, actor, reason, applicable policy, input version, output version, time, result, and error. Every change creates a new version or explicit unavailable state and never rewrites old evidence in place.
