CandidateChunk.title: candidate chunk payload
CandidateChunk.description: Candidate chunk is a retrieval artifact whose defining condition is that it is considered during retrieval and ranking, carrying source provenance, match evidence, score slots, and selection state.

CandidateSet.title: candidate set payload
CandidateSet.description: Candidate set belongs to the Retrieval collection category and differs from broader members in that it is a bounded set of retrieval candidates produced for a query before filtering, scoring, reranking, top-k selection, or context inclusion.

LexicalScore.title: lexical score payload
LexicalScore.description: Lexical score is a retrieval score whose defining condition is that it is a lexical relevance score based on token, term, BM25, TF-IDF, or exact-match evidence between a query and candidate chunk or memory record.

RankFusion.title: rank fusion payload
RankFusion.description: As a ranking operation, Rank fusion is distinguished by the fact that it is a ranking event that combines candidate lists or scores from multiple indexes, models, filters, or retrieval methods into a single ordering.

RankingOperation.title: ranking operation payload
RankingOperation.description: A RankingOperation is an activity with input CandidateSet, score evidence, policy, output ordering or selection, time, and result. It covers RankFusion and TopKSelection and excludes score values, candidate information, and the retrieval query itself.

RerankActivity.title: Rerank activity payload
RerankActivity.description: As a ranking operation, Rerank activity is distinguished by the fact that it compares a candidate set again using an additional model or rule and produces rerank scores retaining model, feature, and version evidence.

RerankScore.title: rerank score payload
RerankScore.description: Rerank score is a retrieval score; its differentiating condition is that it is a score assigned by a reranker model or secondary scoring method after initial retrieval candidate generation.

RetrievalArtifact.title: retrieval artifact payload
RetrievalArtifact.description: As an information record, Retrieval artifact is distinguished by the fact that it is an information artifact with query or run association, provenance, index version, and selection state. It covers RetrievalQuery, CandidateSet, CandidateChunk, RetrievedChunk, and RetrievalTrace. Scores are represented by RetrievalScore, filters by specifications, and RankFusion or TopKSelection by activities.

RetrievalCollection.title: Retrieval collection payload
RetrievalCollection.description: A retrieval collection is a collection organized by a retrieval or ranking activity while retaining member order and scoring context.

RetrievalFilter.title: retrieval filter payload
RetrievalFilter.description: As a specification, Retrieval filter is distinguished by the fact that it is a filter applied to retrieval by namespace, memory type, source, time range, trust zone, permission, freshness, task scope, or exclusion policy.

RetrievalQuery.title: retrieval query payload
RetrievalQuery.description: As a retrieval artifact, Retrieval query is distinguished by the fact that it is a query content, filters, budget, and index version form a replayable RetrievalArtifact; execution is represented by relations rather than conflating information with an event.

query_id.title: retrieval-query ID
query_id.description: Stable identifier for one replayable memory-retrieval query.

query_text.title: query text
query_text.description: Normalized query text submitted to retrieval.

RetrievalRequest.title: Retrieval request payload
RetrievalRequest.description: A retrieval request is an information record binding query, index version, filter conditions, and invocation context into one executable retrieval intent; it does not execute retrieval itself.

request_id.title: retrieval request
request_id.description: Uniquely identifies this retrieval request record.

RetrievalResult.title: Retrieval result record payload
RetrievalResult.description: Retrieval result record belongs to the information record category and differs from broader members in that it retains the candidate set, selected content, scoring context, index version, and diagnostic evidence for one retrieval run.

retrieval_result_id.title: retrieval result
retrieval_result_id.description: Uniquely identifies this retrieval result record.

RetrievalRun.title: Retrieval run payload
RetrievalRun.description: A retrieval run is an activity that fulfills one retrieval request, reads the specified index version, and executes candidate-generation and ranking phases.

retrieval_run_id.title: retrieval run
retrieval_run_id.description: Uniquely identifies this retrieval run record.

RetrievalScore.title: retrieval score payload
RetrievalScore.description: Retrieval score is a discovery score whose defining condition is that it records relevance, confidence, similarity, lexical match, or rerank evidence attached to a retrieved context candidate.

RetrievalTrace.title: retrieval trace payload
RetrievalTrace.description: Retrieval trace belongs to the retrieval artifact category and differs from broader members in that it is a trace record that explains query text, filters, index versions, candidate construction, scores, rank fusion, exclusions, and top-k selection.

RetrievedCandidate.title: retrieved candidate payload
RetrievedCandidate.description: Retrieved candidate is a discovery result; its differentiating condition is that it represents content returned by retrieval before the system decides whether it enters the context package.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

RetrievedChunk.title: retrieved chunk payload
RetrievedChunk.description: Retrieved chunk is a retrieval artifact; its differentiating condition is that it is a chunk returned by retrieval with score, rank, source provenance, index version, and context-inclusion metadata.

RetrievedContextCandidate.title: retrieved context candidate payload
RetrievedContextCandidate.description: As a retrieved candidate, Retrieved context candidate is distinguished by the fact that it represents a retrieved candidate evaluated specifically for inclusion in a model-call or agent-step context package.

rank.title: rank
rank.description: One-based ordering position of the result within one query's candidates.

result_id.title: discovery-result ID
result_id.description: Stable identifier for one lightweight discovery result.

SimilarityScore.title: similarity score payload
SimilarityScore.description: A SimilarityScore is a RetrievalScore that represents semantic similarity between a query and a candidate memory record or chunk, usually derived from embedding distance or vector similarity.

TopKSelection.title: top-k selection payload
TopKSelection.description: As a ranking operation, Top-k selection is distinguished by the fact that it is a RankingOperation selecting candidates under k, budget, diversity, freshness, and policy constraints.

MemoryEntity.title: memory entity payload
MemoryEntity.description: Memory entity is an information record whose defining condition is that it is an information entity with canonical identity, content or carrier, provenance, version, and lifecycle. It covers MemoryRecord, MemoryItem, and MemoryFile. Kind, duration, and scope are orthogonal record-field classifications rather than sibling pseudo-subclasses. MemoryStore, Namespace, Scope, and Policy govern it through relations.

MemoryFile.title: memory file payload
MemoryFile.description: Memory file belongs to the memory entity category and differs from broader members in that it is a file-backed persistent memory resource that preserves project conventions, user preferences, notes, plans, or cross-session context outside the immediate prompt window.

MemoryItem.title: memory item payload
MemoryItem.description: As a memory entity, Memory item is distinguished by the fact that it is an addressable memory unit that may be stored as text, summary, vector-backed chunk, experience, preference, procedure, or reflection.

MemoryNamespace.title: memory namespace payload
MemoryNamespace.description: Memory namespace is a collection whose defining condition is that it is an isolation and lookup boundary that separates memory records by user, task, project, tenant, agent, thread, or cross-session reuse context.

MemoryRecord.title: memory record payload
MemoryRecord.description: As a memory entity, Memory record is distinguished by the fact that it is a persistent or session-scoped memory entry with content, provenance, type, namespace, scope, retention state, confidence, and lifecycle history.

memory_scope.title: memory scope
memory_scope.description: Composable session, task, or cross-session applicability scopes.

memory_kind.title: memory kind
memory_kind.description: Composable tags for episodic, semantic, procedural, reflective, preference, or experience kind.

memory_duration.title: Memory duration
memory_duration.description: Identifies a MemoryRecord's retention and use duration, such as working, short-term, or long-term; this dimension is independent of kind and scope.

memory_record_id.title: memory-record ID
memory_record_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical memory record across immutable versions.

version.title: version
version.description: Monotonic value identifying one immutable version of the memory record.

valid_from.title: valid from
valid_from.description: UTC instant from which this version is valid for reasoning or context assembly.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: UTC instant after which this version is no longer valid; null for the current version.

confidence.title: confidence
confidence.description: Zero-to-one confidence assigned when the record was written or validated.

provenance_ref.title: provenance reference
provenance_ref.description: Points to the immutable source and location supporting the memory content.

access_policy_id.title: access-policy ID
access_policy_id.description: Identifies the policy governing read, update, and disclosure of this record.

MemoryRetentionPolicy.title: memory retention policy payload
MemoryRetentionPolicy.description: Memory retention policy belongs to the specification category and differs from broader members in that it is a policy that governs how long memory records, summaries, indexes, and traces may be retained, refreshed, compacted, expired, deleted, or disclosed.

MemoryScope.title: memory scope payload
MemoryScope.description: Memory scope is a specification; its differentiating condition is that it is a policy boundary that states where memory may be read, written, updated, reused, disclosed, or forgotten across session, user, task, tenant, and trust contexts.

MemoryStore.title: memory store payload
MemoryStore.description: Memory store is an entity whose defining condition is that it is a storage anchor that supports reading, writing, updating, deleting, merging, compacting, expiring, retrieving, and auditing memory records across session or long-term scopes.

deployment_scope.title: deployment scope
deployment_scope.description: States whether the store endpoint is deployed locally, remotely, or in a hybrid arrangement; the facet does not change MemoryStore identity.

MemoryTombstone.title: Memory tombstone record payload
MemoryTombstone.description: As a memory record, Memory tombstone record is distinguished by the fact that it is an immutable memory record replacing a deleted, expired, or evicted version while retaining original record identity, termination reason, operation, and time without retaining withdrawn content.

memory_scope.title: memory scope
memory_scope.description: Composable session, task, or cross-session applicability scopes.

memory_kind.title: memory kind
memory_kind.description: Composable tags for episodic, semantic, procedural, reflective, preference, or experience kind.

memory_duration.title: Memory duration
memory_duration.description: Identifies a MemoryRecord's retention and use duration, such as working, short-term, or long-term; this dimension is independent of kind and scope.

memory_record_id.title: memory-record ID
memory_record_id.description: Stable identifier for the logical memory record across immutable versions.

version.title: version
version.description: Monotonic value identifying one immutable version of the memory record.

valid_from.title: valid from
valid_from.description: UTC instant from which this version is valid for reasoning or context assembly.

valid_until.title: valid until
valid_until.description: UTC instant after which this version is no longer valid; null for the current version.

confidence.title: confidence
confidence.description: Zero-to-one confidence assigned when the record was written or validated.

provenance_ref.title: provenance reference
provenance_ref.description: Points to the immutable source and location supporting the memory content.

access_policy_id.title: access-policy ID
access_policy_id.description: Identifies the policy governing read, update, and disclosure of this record.
